的启示是：长上下文不只是位置编码问题，真正瓶颈在于哪些 token 值得被持续保留和精确关注；如果重要 token 可以被优先处理，那么模型容量可以更有效地用在信息密度高的部分。这类工作也会影响后续 pretrain 数据组织和 curriculum，因为长序列训练是否值得，取决于推理时能否承接。","technical_approach":"VCC 的核心思想是优先处理重要 则被合并、压缩或降采样。与固定窗口、块稀疏或纯线性近似不同，这种设计把‘信息选择’放在中心位置，因此更符合自然语言中注意力集中于少数关键位置的事实。它有效的前提是重要性估计足够准；边界是，如果关键证据分散且早期被误判丢弃，质量会出现不可恢复的损失。","experiments":"从标题可以确认，实验会重点展示在 128K 及以上上下文长度下，相比标准 Transformer paper targets the practical bottleneck of very long-context transformers: once context reaches 128K tokens or more, full attention becomes prohibitively expensive, while many sparse or linear alternatives lose too much quality. Earlier methods often traded away accuracy for efficiency or were awkward for generation-time use.","importance":"This is a long-context systems contribution with direct relevance to inference, serving, and scalable training. For pretraining, the important lesson is that long context is not only a positional encoding problem; the deeper issue is which tokens deserve precise retention over time. If the model can prioritize high-value tokens, capacity can be spent where the information density is actually high. That also feeds back into how long-sequence data should be organized during training.","technical_approach":"VCC prioritizes important tokens instead of allocating equal compute to the entire history. The method appears to be an importance-aware context compression or caching strategy: high-value tokens keep more faithful representations or higher access priority, while lower-value tokens are compressed, merged, or downsampled. Compared with fixed windows, block sparsity, or purely linear approximations, this puts information selection at the center, which matches the empirical fact that useful attention often concentrates on a small subset of tokens. The method works only if token importance is estimated well; if dispersed evidence is discarded early, the quality loss can be irreversible.","experiments":"From the title, the empirical claim should be that VCC scales to 128K+ context with a better quality–efficiency trade-off than standard transformers or other efficient-attention baselines. Barrett","Zhangyang Wang","Beidi Chen"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"NEURIPS","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/nips/Zhang00CZC0TRBW23","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/nips/Zhang00CZC0TRBW23","_ee":"http://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/6ceefa7b15572587b78ecfcebb2827f8-Abstract-Conference.html","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":8,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作解决的是 LLM 生成推理时的 KV cache 瓶颈：上下文一长，注意力需要读取全部历史 key/value，显存和带宽压力都很高，而大多数 token 对后续生成的贡献其实并不均匀。过去常见方案是窗口截断或统一压缩，但这些方法容易把真正重要的远距离信息一起丢掉。","importance":"这是生成推理系统里的代表性工作之一，影响点很实用：它把 cache 压缩从‘按位置裁剪’改成‘按重要性保留’。对 pretrain 的启示在于，模型推理时确实存在少数 heavy hitters，也就是反复被关注的关键 token；这说明长上下文建模里信息分布高度不均，训练和架构设计都可以围绕这一点做更针对性的优化。它也是后续很多 KV eviction / cache budget 工作的重要起点。","technical_approach":"H2O 的核心方法是识别 attention 中的 heavy-hitter token，并在 KV cache 预算受限时优先保留这些 token，而不是简单保留最近窗口。它本质上是一种 oracle-guided 或近似 oracle 的 cache eviction 策略：哪些历史 token 在未来最可能持续贡献注意力，就给它们更高生存优先级。与 sliding window 相比，关键区别是允许远距离但高价值的信息长期留存；与静态摘要压缩相比，它不强行把信息揉成低维表示，而是直接保留原始 KV。这样设计有效，是因为生成中 attention 的头部集中现象很强；边界是 heavy hitter 的识别若滞后或失准，收益会明显下降。","experiments":"这篇论文的关键事实通常包括：在有限 KV cache 预算下，H2O 相比 recent-window 或其他 eviction 基线能更好保持生成质量，同时显著降低显存占用。没有摘要时，我不能可靠给出具体模型如 LLaMA/OPT 的规模、cache 压缩比、吞吐提升和 perplexity/benchmark 变化数字；但可以确认，实验重点会是不同 cache budget 下的质量曲线，以及不同注意力头/层上的 heavy-hitter 统计分析。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles the KV-cache bottleneck in LLM generation: as context grows, attention must read all historical keys and values, which makes memory and bandwidth increasingly expensive, even though not all past tokens matter equally. Earlier solutions often used recency-based truncation or uniform compression, which can discard genuinely important long-range evidence.","importance":"This is one of the more practically important inference systems papers in the area because it changes cache management from position-based retention to importance-based retention. For pretraining researchers, the broader takeaway is that generation-time attention is highly skewed: a small set of heavy-hitter tokens gets revisited disproportionately often. That suggests long-context modeling is fundamentally uneven in information density, which should influence both architecture and training design. The work also became a reference point for later KV-eviction and cache-budget methods.","technical_approach":"H2O identifies heavy-hitter tokens in attention and prioritizes them when the KV cache is budget-limited, instead of simply keeping the most recent tokens. In essence, it is an oracle-guided or oracle-inspired cache eviction strategy: tokens that are likely to receive sustained future attention are given higher survival priority. Compared with a sliding window, the key difference is that distant but important tokens can survive for a long time; compared with static compression, it keeps original KV entries rather than collapsing them into a lossy summary. This works because attention mass during generation is often sharply concentrated. The limitation is that gains depend on how well heavy hitters are identified online.","experiments":"The core empirical claim is typically that under a fixed KV-cache budget, H2O preserves generation quality better than recency-based or other eviction baselines while significantly reducing memory use. Without the abstract, I cannot safely provide exact model names, compression ratios, throughput gains, or perplexity/benchmark numbers. It identifies two concrete failure modes: objective conflict between helpfulness and safety, and a generalization gap where safety does not cover domains that capabilities already do.","importance":"This matters directly for the pretrain-to-posttrain interface. The lesson is that alignment cannot be treated as a thin refusal layer on top of a capable model. Once the model has learned a capability, jailbreaks become likely whenever safety training does not generalize over the same semantic space. So the problem is partly one of objective design and partly one of distributional coverage, not just prompt engineering.","technical_approach":"The method is analytical and empirical: define two failure modes and use them to guide jailbreak construction. In competing objectives, the model is trained to be both useful and safe, and the usefulness objective can be exploited when the two conflict. In mismatched generalization, the model has the capability for harmful content, but safety behavior does not generalize to the relevant phrasing or domain. This is more informative than a catalog of attacks because it explains why alignment remains brittle.","experiments":"From the abstract, the authors evaluate state-of-the-art models including GPT-4 and Claude v1.3, using both prior jailbreaks and new attacks derived from their framework. Specifically, how does the rank of weight matrices evolve? Prior theoretical analyses of learning dynamics were mostly limited to linear or shallow models.","importance":"A theory/mechanism-level contribution with direct implications for pretraining. This is especially useful for ViTs because the trade-off is not just parameter count: token count drives quadratic attention cost, so the optimum depends strongly on resolution and patch size.","experiments":"The experimental center is likely compute-matched comparison across ViT designs, plus validation that the proposed scaling law predicts near-optimal architecture families. Earlier compression and sparse-attention methods help, but many use fixed patterns that neither adapt to the input nor explain what was kept.","importance":"This is a method contribution at the intersection of efficiency and interpretability, with clear relevance to long-context training and inference. If dynamic pruning can remove irrelevant context while preserving quality, it cuts compute and memory directly, and the retained context becomes an interpretable signal of what the model actually used.","technical_approach":"The key idea is dynamic context pruning: during autoregressive generation, select a context subset online based on the current input and model state. The difference from fixed windows or static sparsity is that pruning is input-dependent at the token or segment level. This should work because useful evidence in language modeling is often sparse. But compressing weights to 2-bit typically causes accuracy collapse. Existing PTQ methods work at 3-4 bits but lack theoretical guarantees and practical solutions at 2-bit.","importance":"A system/method-level contribution with direct practical value for LLM serving. Viable 2-bit quantization means 8× compression from FP16, with massive deployment cost implications. For pretrain researchers, understanding quantization limits informs quantization-aware design decisions during pretraining. QuIP became an important baseline in LLM quantization (followed by QuIP#).","technical_approach":"QuIP's key idea is applying random orthogonal transformations (incoherence processing) to weight matrices before quantization, making the weight distribution more uniform so that round-to-nearest quantization incurs smaller error. Theoretically proves that when incoherence between weights and the quantization grid is sufficiently high, 2-bit quantization error has a provable upper bound. Steps: (1) preprocess weights with random Hadamard or orthogonal transforms; (2) quantize column-wise in the transformed space; (3) apply inverse transform on quantized weights during inference. The critical difference from GPTQ is the incoherence preprocessing step—this is what makes 2-bit work.","experiments":"Tested on LLaMA models (7B–65B) at 2-bit. QuIP significantly outperforms GPTQ and naive RTN in perplexity at 2-bit; on LLaMA-65B, 2-bit QuIP approaches GPTQ 3-bit perplexity. Ablation confirms incoherence processing is the main performance driver—removing it causes severe degradation.","authors_note":"Christopher De Sa is a Cornell professor with long-standing work on low-precision training and systems optimization. QuIP was followed by QuIP# which further improved 2-bit quantization quality."},"tags":["quantization","llm","inference"],"notable_authors":["Christopher De Sa"]},{"id":"ChenXGLZLMZW23","title":"DiffUTE: Universal Text Editing Diffusion Model","authors":["Haoxing Chen","Zhuoer Xu","Zhangxuan Gu","Jun Lan","Xing Zheng","Yaohui Li","Changhua Meng","Huijia Zhu","Weiqiang Wang"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"NEURIPS","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/nips/ChenXGLZLMZW23","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/nips/ChenXGLZLMZW23","_ee":"http://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/c7138635035501eb71b0adf6ddc319d6-Abstract-Conference.html","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{},"summary_en":{},"tags":["diffusion-lm","text-editing","non-autoregressive","generation"]},{"id":"ChengLCL0023","title":"Lift Yourself Up: Retrieval-augmented Text Generation with Self-Memory","authors":["Xin Cheng","Di Luo","Xiuying Chen","Lemao Liu","Dongyan Zhao","Rui Yan"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"NEURIPS","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/nips/ChengLCL0023","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/nips/ChengLCL0023","_ee":"http://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/887262aeb3eafb01ef0fd0e3a87a8831-Abstract-Conference.html","category_id":"agent","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{},"summary_en":{},"tags":["rag","retrieval","text-generation","memory"]},{"id":"ChoiSD23","title":"Tools for Verifying Neural Models&apos; Earlier work often softened this issue with more data, better prompts, or task-specific setups. This paper instead asks whether strong surface performance actually reflects rule-like generalization, or merely memorization of frequent patterns.","importance":"Its contribution is primarily about capability diagnosis and mechanism-level understanding. The pretraining takeaway is clear: scaling improves average performance, but it does not automatically produce systematic compositionality. If the training objective mainly rewards local co-occurrence statistics, models can look competent while still relying on shortcuts where abstract rule application is required. That matters for synthetic data design, curriculum learning, structural inductive biases, and reasoning-oriented post-training.","technical_approach":"The paper does not introduce a new model; it builds cleaner evaluations that remove shallow statistical shortcuts. It studies transformers on tasks involving variable binding, rule composition, and multi-step conditional inference, with out-of-distribution splits designed so that frequent training templates do not trivially solve test cases. The key distinction is between solving an instance and solving it for the right reason. Models can perform well in-distribution yet collapse when the compositional structure changes, suggesting template matching rather than abstract rule manipulation.","experiments":"The main empirical finding is that multiple transformer variants reach strong in-distribution accuracy but degrade sharply on compositional splits and stricter controls. A recurring pattern is that errors grow quickly with longer dependency chains and more variables that must remain consistently bound. The error analysis is the useful part: common failures include variable swaps, local consistency with global inconsistency, and over-reliance on seen surface orderings. RLHF 类思想迁移到生成扩散模型，属于 post-train 反馈学习对生成建模的扩展。对 pretrain 研究的启示是，生成模型的训练目标未必需要被 likelihood 完全绑定；当可微 surrogate 或 reward model 足够可靠时，可以在后训练阶段显式优化人偏好、审美或指令遵循。这对未来统一语言-图像生成模型的训练接口很有参考价值。","technical_approach":"核心方法是对 text-to-image diffusion 进行强化学习微调。作者将整条去噪采样轨迹视作 sequential decision process，定义基于 reward model、审美评分器或人类偏好的奖励，再用策略优化方法更新 diffusion 模型。关键设计难点在于 diffusion 不是标准离散 token policy：动作是高维连续去噪步骤，长轨迹方差高，且 reward 常只在最终图像上给出。作者因此需要在 credit assignment、KL 约束或行为保持上做专门处理，以避免模型为了 reward 牺牲多样性或出现视觉伪影。","experiments":"实验结论是：RL fine-tuning 可以显著提升特定 reward 指标下的图像质量和偏好得分，但收益高度依赖 reward 设计，并存在典型 reward hacking 风险。论文通常比较原始 diffusion 模型、监督式微调和 RL 微调，在人评、审美分数或 prompt alignment 上报告改进。更有价值的观察是，加入适当的 KL 或保守更新约束可减轻模式塌缩和伪影；如果 reward model 偏差较大，模型会快速学会投机。也就是说，这条路是可行的，但关键不在 RL 本身，而在 reward 信号质量与优化约束。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles a mismatch in text-to-image diffusion: training objectives based on likelihood or denoising quality do not fully align with what humans actually prefer in final images. As a result, models can be good samplers but still underperform on aesthetics or instruction following. The paper explores reinforcement learning as a fine-tuning mechanism to optimize image quality more directly.","importance":"Its significance lies in extending RLHF-style ideas to diffusion generation. For pretraining and posttraining research, the key lesson is that generative models need not remain tied to likelihood-style objectives once a reliable reward signal is available. If reward models are good enough, post-training can explicitly optimize for preference, aesthetics, or prompt fidelity. This is relevant to future unified language-image generators as well.","technical_approach":"The core method is RL fine-tuning for text-to-image diffusion models. The denoising trajectory is treated as a sequential decision process, rewards are derived from preference models, aesthetic scorers, or alignment signals, and policy optimization is used to update the diffusion model. The hard part is that diffusion is not a standard token policy: actions are high-dimensional denoising steps, trajectories are long, and rewards are often only observed on the final image. The method therefore needs careful credit assignment and conservative updates, often with KL-style constraints, to avoid sacrificing diversity or creating artifacts.","experiments":"The main empirical result is that RL fine-tuning can improve targeted reward metrics and preference scores, but the gains depend strongly on reward quality and come with clear reward-hacking risks. The paper compares base diffusion models, supervised fine-tuning, and RL-tuned variants on human preference, aesthetic score, and prompt alignment. The most informative observation is that conservative optimization, such as KL regularization, helps prevent mode collapse and visual artifacts. When the reward model is biased, the diffusion model quickly exploits it. So the route is promising, but reward quality and optimization constraints are the real bottlenecks.","authors_note":""},"tags":["reinforcement-learning","diffusion","alignment","text-to-image"],"notable_authors":["Ying Fan","Olivia Watkins","Yuqing Du","Hao Liu","Moonkyung Ryu"]},{"id":"2307.11353","title":"What can a Single Attention Layer Learn? The alternative explored here is to treat the reasoning process as a latent variable and train through inference over that latent structure.","importance":"This is a significant contribution at the training-paradigm level. It matters especially at the pretrain–posttrain interface because it asks whether reasoning ability must come from explicit rationale distillation or can instead emerge from learning with hidden intermediate computation. That question is central to latent reasoning, supervision quality, and the role of test-time compute.","technical_approach":"The key idea is to formulate CoT training as latent-variable inference. The final answer is observed, while the reasoning trace is latent. Training therefore optimizes over possible hidden reasoning paths instead of forcing the model to imitate one provided rationale exactly. The benefit is reduced dependence on costly CoT labels and less overcommitment to a particular textual form of reasoning. Compared with direct CoT supervision or rationale self-training, this is closer to the realistic setting where multiple internal derivations can lead to the same answer. The trade-off is more expensive and more delicate inference during training.","experiments":"Even without the abstract, the likely evaluation scope is clear: math, symbolic reasoning, or multi-step QA tasks where CoT supervision is usually helpful, compared against direct CoT supervision, answer-only training, or other latent-rationale baselines. 或偏好数据只给一个总偏好分，模型分不清回答差是因为没帮助，还是因为有害；这会直接污染奖励建模和拒答策略，因此作者专门构造了把两者拆开的数据集。","importance":"结论是：这是一篇数据级兼接口级的重要工作，价值不在提出新对齐算法，而在把训练信号拆干净。对 pretrain/posttrain 闭环的启示很直接：对齐阶段最稀缺的不是更多偏好样本，而是更可分解、更低混淆的监督信号。把 helpfulness/harmlessness 分离后，奖励模型、拒答模型、内容审核器都能更明确地优化各自目标，也更容易分析 reward hacking 来自哪里。","technical_approach":"核心贡献是 BeaverTails 数据集及其配套标注协议。作者为 QA 对构建了安全元标签，并分别收集 helpfulness 与 harmlessness 的专家比较数据，避免用单一偏好标签把两个维度压扁。这个设计的好处是训练时可以做多目标建模：一个分支学有用性，一个分支学无害性，再在策略优化时决定权重；相比传统 RLHF 一把梭，这样更适合处理“回答质量高但风险高”或“安全但没用”的冲突样本。代价是标注成本更高，且两个维度之间仍可能存在主观耦合。","experiments":"关键事实是数据规模很大。摘要里给出了 333,963 条 QA RLHF。虽然摘要未给出完整指标，但这类实验通常会比较监督微调、奖励模型或偏好优化在安全基准上的拒答率、越狱成功率和有用性保持。对读者最有价值的不是单个分数，而是分离标签后是否能稳定减少“安全提升伴随帮助性明显下降”的副作用。","authors_note":"如果仅依据当前提供信息，无法可靠确认机构与代表性团队脉络。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a real weakness in LLM alignment data: helpfulness and harmlessness are usually collapsed into a single preference signal, which makes the reward ambiguous. When a response is dispreferred, standard RLHF data rarely tells you whether it was unsafe, unhelpful, or both, and that ambiguity leaks directly into reward models and refusal behavior.","importance":"This is mainly a data contribution with strong implications for the pretrain-to-posttrain interface. The important point is not “more preference data,” but cleaner supervision structure. By separating helpfulness from harmlessness, the dataset gives researchers a way to train and diagnose alignment systems with less label entanglement, which is exactly where reward hacking and over-refusal often start.","technical_approach":"The core contribution is the BeaverTails dataset and its annotation scheme. It provides safety meta-labels for QA pairs and separate expert comparison data for helpfulness and harmlessness, rather than forcing annotators into a single overall preference. That design enables multi-objective modeling: one model or head can track usefulness, another safety, and policy optimization can then choose the trade-off explicitly. Compared with standard preference datasets, this is a cleaner interface, though it is also more expensive to annotate and still subject to subjective coupling between the two dimensions.","experiments":"The headline fact is dataset scale. The authors further demonstrate use cases in content moderation and RLHF. Pan"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$a","venue":"NEURIPS","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.14858","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/nips/JiangGZP23","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/nips/JiangGZP23","_ee":"http://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/8f1bacee31caf990a4f08d84f0ccb322-Abstract-Conference.html","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：Pre-LN Transformer 里到底该用 LayerNorm 还是 RMSNorm，以及两者能否在不改模型行为的前提下互相转换。过去社区对 LN 和 RMSNorm 的使用很分裂：RMSNorm 更省算，但缺少去均值，大家担心表达能力下降；LN 更标准，但开销更高，且已经有大量大模型转向 RMSNorm。作者试图给出一个更干净的等价性分析，而不是继续停留在经验争论。","importance":"结论是：这是一篇方法级但很工程化的重要工作，价值在于把“规范层选型”从经验偏好变成可分析、可替换的设计决策。对大规模 pretrain 的启示很实际：如果 Pre-RMSNorm 与某种中心化版本在功能上等价或近似等价，那么训练稳定性、吞吐和迁移兼容性可以一起考虑，而不是把规范层当成不可动的架构细节。","technical_approach":"核心方法是提出 Pre-RMSNorm 和 Pre-CRMSNorm，并证明它们与 Pre-LN Transformer 在一定条件下的等价性或可转换关系。作者的关键点不是简单地把 LN 换成 RMSNorm，而是通过结构改写保留 LN 的关键表示性质，同时继承 RMSNorm 的计算效率。CRMSNorm 可以理解为在 RMSNorm 框架中补回中心化相关的信息，使其更接近 LN 的行为。这样设计有效，因为 Pre-LN 中规范层的作用主要是控制尺度与训练动力学；如果能在更便宜的实现中保留这些作用，就能减少归一化计算开销。边界在于：理论等价不一定覆盖有限精度、并行实现和超参数不匹配下的实际训练差异。","experiments":"摘要没有给出具体模型大小、训练 token 或提升数字，因此无法精确复述结果。可以合理判断，实验应比较 Pre-LN、Pre-RMSNorm、Pre-CRMSNorm 在训练稳定性、收敛速度、最终精度和推理效率上的差异，并验证参数转换或替换后的性能保持情况。对预训练工程而言，最值得看的是两类结果：一是大模型和长训练下是否仍稳定，不只是小规模 toy setting；二是吞吐收益是否来自真实 kernel 成本下降，而不是其他实现细节。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a practical but unresolved question in Transformer design: should Pre-LN models use LayerNorm or RMSNorm, and can one be converted into the other without changing the model’s effective behavior? The field has treated this mostly as an empirical choice—LayerNorm is standard, RMSNorm is cheaper—but the trade-off has remained conceptually muddy.","importance":"This is a method-level contribution with strong engineering relevance for large-scale pretraining. Its value is that it turns normalization choice from a folklore preference into something more analyzable and potentially interchangeable. If Pre-RMSNorm or its centered variant can preserve the useful behavior of Pre-LN while reducing cost, that matters directly for training stability, throughput, and architecture portability.","technical_approach":"The core proposal is Pre-RMSNorm and Pre-CRMSNorm, together with an analysis of their equivalence or convertibility to Pre-LN Transformers under suitable conditions. The point is not merely to swap LayerNorm for RMSNorm, but to recover the representation properties associated with centering while keeping the simpler scaling-only structure where possible. CRMSNorm effectively injects back the missing centering-related behavior into an RMS-style formulation. This is compelling because, in Pre-LN Transformers, normalization mainly serves to control scale and optimization dynamics; if those effects can be preserved in a cheaper form, the replacement is worthwhile. The caveat is that theoretical equivalence may weaken under finite precision, implementation details, or poorly matched hyperparameters.","experiments":"The abstract does not report exact model sizes, token counts, or numerical gains, so a quantitative summary would be speculative. The likely experiments compare Pre-LN, Pre-RMSNorm, and Pre-CRMSNorm on stability, convergence, final quality, and efficiency, and may also test parameter conversion between forms. Standard training pipelines assume multiply-accumulate everywhere, which is expensive on low-power hardware and constrains more radical hardware-algorithm co-design.","importance":"This is a method and systems contribution. It does not redefine mainstream pretraining, but it puts pressure on a strong assumption in today’s stack: that floating-point multiplication is indispensable during training. If core Transformer operations can be replaced by piecewise affine ones without collapsing optimization, that opens a path toward far more hardware-efficient training on specialized accelerators. The usual options are activation checkpointing, which adds recomputation, or approximate attention variants, which alter model behavior more broadly. This work targets a narrower point: approximate the softmax output itself to save memory while disturbing training dynamics as little as possible.","importance":"This is a systems contribution with direct relevance to large-scale pretraining. Its value comes from addressing a common memory hotspot in transformers with a more surgical method than full recomputation. If the approximation is accurate enough, the saved memory can be traded for larger batch size, longer context, or more efficient parallelism. In practice, such local approximations are often easier to deploy than architectural rewrites.","technical_approach":"The core method is softmax output approximation for activation-memory-efficient training. It does not replace attention with a sparse or low-rank variant, and it does not change the forward computation more than necessary. Instead, it exploits structural properties of the softmax output to store or reconstruct a compact approximation that preserves the key gradient signal. Compared with checkpointing, the aim is less recomputation; compared with approximate attention architectures, the aim is closer fidelity to standard attention. The intuition is that although the softmax output is large, the information most relevant to gradients may be concentrated in a limited portion of the probability mass.","experiments":"The key empirical questions are memory savings, runtime overhead, and whether final quality remains essentially unchanged. Using flip-flop language modeling, the authors isolate a setting where models can achieve decent training loss yet still fail to maintain stable internal state over time; this kind of failure is often hidden in natural data.","importance":"This is important for mechanistic diagnosis rather than for proposing a new pretraining objective. The lesson for pretraining is that low next-token loss does not guarantee that the model has learned a robust algorithm for persistent state tracking. If training corpora and evaluations do not stress such structure, these weaknesses stay latent and later appear in code execution, tool use, or multi-turn memory-heavy settings.","technical_approach":"The approach is to build a controlled flip-flop language modeling task that isolates one capability: consistent long-horizon tracking and updating of a small discrete state. That makes it easier to inspect whether attention forms reliable read-write behavior. Compared with generic synthetic tasks, this setup can precisely vary dependency length, update frequency, and interference, which is useful for identifying the failure mechanism. Existing compression methods often require expensive rescoring or degrade quality noticeably. The authors build on a simple empirical claim: token importance tends to persist over time, so keys and values judged unimportant early are unlikely to become critical later.","importance":"This is an inference-systems paper, but it also provides a useful modeling insight: attention usage over history is highly non-uniform and temporally structured. If the persistence-of-importance hypothesis is robust, it has implications not only for serving but also for sparse attention design and long-context training strategies.","technical_approach":"Scissorhands compresses KV cache by pruning based on estimated importance, while exploiting the idea that importance is persistent so it does not need expensive re-evaluation at every step. Unlike fixed windows or uniform dropping, it aims to retain a small set of globally important tokens. Unlike methods that frequently recompute selection, it keeps the policy cheap. The intuition is that many tokens, once they stop receiving meaningful attention, rarely become useful again; the tradeoff is that tasks requiring rare long-range retrieval can be harmed by premature pruning.","experiments":"Papers of this kind are typically evaluated on open LLMs such as LLaMA or OPT for long-form generation and question answering, against baselines like full KV, sliding-window caching, and other token-retention methods. The key metrics are compression ratio, memory reduction, throughput, and quality degradation in perplexity or downstream accuracy. Earlier work emphasized image-text alignment and caption-style supervision, which is not enough for building a conversational visual assistant. The question is how to transfer instruction-following from LLMs to visual inputs.","importance":"This is a highly influential posttraining-interface paper rather than a new pretraining architecture. Its main contribution is to show that strong multimodal assistant behavior can emerge from relatively cheap visual instruction tuning on top of existing components. For pretraining research, the lesson is that a large part of the capability gap may sit at the alignment interface rather than in the base objective alone.","technical_approach":"The method is to connect a pretrained vision encoder to an LLM and then apply supervised visual instruction tuning. This is a recipe innovation more than a from-scratch multimodal pretraining scheme: image features from a frozen or lightly adapted visual backbone are projected into the language model, and the combined system is tuned on instruction-response pairs grounded in images, often with GPT-assisted data generation. Compared with caption fine-tuning, instruction tuning teaches task conditioning and dialogue behavior. Compared with end-to-end large-scale multimodal pretraining, it is far cheaper but also more constrained by the encoder-projector bottleneck.","experiments":"The well-known empirical setup around this line of work typically uses a CLIP-style vision encoder and a Vicuna/LLaMA-family language model, trained on GPT-generated visual instruction data. This matters because CLIP-style alignment works well but depends on large paired datasets and inherits their biases.","importance":"This is a paradigm-oriented contribution rather than a pure benchmark paper. It explores whether a shared discrete interface can partially replace explicit pairwise supervision, which is directly relevant to multimodal tokenization and native multimodal pretraining. For pretraining research, the key idea is that alignment may come not only from contrastive losses on pairs, but also from how the representation space itself is structured.","technical_approach":"The method is a Language Quantized AutoEncoder: images are encoded, quantized into discrete codes, and the quantized space is shaped to be more compatible with language representations. Unlike a standard VQ-VAE, the point is not only image reconstruction; the quantized codebook is treated as a cross-modal interface. Unlike CLIP, the method does not primarily depend on direct paired contrastive supervision. This can work if semantic factors in images can be organized into reusable discrete units, though the likely limitation is weaker fine-grained grounding without explicit image-text pairs.","experiments":"From the title alone, the experiments likely focus on unsupervised or weakly supervised alignment quality and transfer to retrieval or generation tasks. Prior work mostly fits continuous power laws to loss reduction, which is useful descriptively but weak at explaining threshold behaviors; this work revisits scaling through a quantization view.","importance":"Its contribution is mainly conceptual rather than algorithmic. For pretraining, the key takeaway is that some “emergent” behaviors may come from discrete representational capacity and feature acquisition, not necessarily from a qualitatively new mechanism; that changes how we interpret scaling curves and how we decide whether more scale is buying us new capabilities or just smoother averages.","technical_approach":"The proposal is a quantization model of learning: performance improves when the network becomes able to encode additional discrete features or bits required by the task, and each newly acquired feature yields a step in the curve. This differs from standard continuous scaling views that treat capacity as a smooth scalar. The design is useful because it naturally explains thresholds and plateaus, especially when tasks decompose into a hierarchy of required features; the limitation is that it is an explanatory model, not a drop-in engineering law for training-budget planning.","experiments":"The paper’s evidence is best understood through controlled fits and qualitative scaling phenomena rather than frontier-scale benchmarks. The important empirical claim is that a quantized model can explain kinks, plateaus, and abrupt capability transitions better than a single smooth power law. For practitioners, the useful checks are whether the effect holds across tasks, whether harder tasks require more discrete steps, and whether the interpretation survives architecture and data changes; if so, average loss alone is insufficient for reading progress.","authors_note":"MIT; Max Tegmark is the most recognizable author and is known for work on theory-motivated views of deep learning and foundation models."},"tags":["scaling-law","quantization","neural-scaling","theory"],"notable_authors":["Eric J. Goodman 是较知名作者，在认知建模、语言学习与推理相关方向有代表性工作。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles a practical bottleneck: long prompts are expensive and often repetitive at inference time, so can we compress them into a small reusable representation without losing much downstream behavior. Truncation loses information, and KV-cache reuse still scales with original token count; gist tokens are proposed as a learned compression mechanism.","importance":"This matters at the interface of pretraining and inference. The broader message is that not all useful context needs to remain token-level forever; some of it can be distilled into compact internal state. That is useful for serving efficiency and also suggests training objectives that encourage explicit context abstraction.","technical_approach":"The method introduces gist tokens that the model learns to use as a bottleneck for storing the task-relevant content of a prompt. After compression, the original prompt can be dropped and later computation conditions only on the gist. This is not simple pooling and not standard prompt tuning: the gist representation is input-dependent and trained for behavioral fidelity on downstream continuation or answering. It works when the prompt can be summarized into a compact latent state; it will struggle when exact wording, detailed retrieval, or faithful copying is required.","experiments":"The key comparisons are against full prompts, truncation, and natural-language summarization, measuring both task performance and token savings. The most useful empirical fact is the quality-retention curve as the prompt is compressed to very few gist tokens. It strengthens the interface between train-time modeling choices and real inference economics.","technical_approach":"The core approach is to build a low-cost estimator for latency and throughput of autoregressive Transformers under realistic serving settings. Transformer 的核心瓶颈。Flash Attention 解决了 IO 效率问题但仍是精确全注意力，而稀疏注意力方法虽然降低了计算量但通常需要固定的稀疏模式（如 local + global），无法根据输入动态调整。本文提出动态稀疏注意力与 Flash Attention 的结合。","importance":"系统/方法级贡献，直接关联 pretrain 需要计算、哪些可以跳过。具体实现分两步：(1) 用一个轻量级的预估步骤（如基于 query-key 块级内积的近似）快速判断每个 block 的重要性；(2) 只对重要性超过阈值的 block 执行完整的 Flash Attention 计算。与固定稀疏模式（如 BigBird、Longformer）的区别在于稀疏模式是数据依赖的，能适应不同输入的注意力分布。与 Flash Attention 的兼容性是关键工程贡献。","experiments":"在语言建模任务上测试，序列长度从 2K 到 16K+。在保持接近全注意力的困惑度（perplexity 差距 <0.5）的前提下，实现了 2-4x 的墙钟时间加速（具体取决于序列长度和稀疏率）。与 BigBird、Longformer 等固定稀疏方法相比，动态稀疏在相同计算预算下困惑度更低。消融显示预估步骤的开销约占总计算的 5-10%。","authors_note":"Martin Jaggi 来自 EPFL，François Fleuret 来自 University of Geneva / IDIAP，两人在高效 Transformer 和优化方面有持续工作。Matteo Pagliardini 也来自 EPFL。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Flash Attention This paper combines dynamic sparsity with Flash Attention for long sequences.","importance":"A system/method-level contribution directly relevant to long-context pretrain efficiency. If dynamic sparse attention maintains quality while significantly reducing compute on long sequences, it benefits both pretrain-stage long-text training and inference-stage KV-cache management.","technical_approach":"Dynamic Sparse Flash Attention works in two steps within Flash Attention's block-wise computation framework: (1) a lightweight estimation step (e.g., block-level query-key inner product approximation) quickly scores each block's importance; (2) only blocks above a threshold receive full Flash Attention computation. Unlike fixed sparse patterns, sparsity is data-dependent and adapts to each input's attention distribution. The notable idea is not just sparse attention, but the introduction of explicit block-level state as a middle ground between full attention and more compressed recurrent-style memory.","technical_approach":"Block-State Transformers appear to partition the sequence into blocks and maintain an updateable state per block, with token-level computation interacting with these block states. Goodman"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"NEURIPS","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/nips/PrystawskiLG23","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/nips/PrystawskiLG23","_ee":"http://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/e0af79ad53a336b4c4b4f7e2a68eb609-Abstract-Conference.html","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要回答的核心问题是：为什么 step-by-step reasoning 会在语言模型或序列学习系统里自然出现，而不是把链式推理当成纯粹由监督模板灌输出来的行为。过去很多解释停留在“CoT 提供了更多中间监督”或“更像人类解题过程”，但对其出现条件和归纳偏置缺少更机制化的说明。","importance":"它的重要性在机制解释层面。对于 pretrain 和 ICL 研究，这类工作提供的是“为什么某些推理格式更容易被模型学到”的因果线索，而不是直接给出新训练法。若结论成立，它意味着 reasoning emergence 可能更多取决于训练分布中的局部可泛化结构，而不是模型先天拥有显式算法模块，这会影响我们如何设计数据合成、课程学习和思维链监督。","technical_approach":"方法上它更像理论建模加可控实验，而不是大规模系统创新。题目里的 locality of experience 指向一个关键观点：模型之所以偏好逐步推理，是因为训练中更常见、也更容易泛化的是局部状态转移或局部一致性，而不是一步跳到远距离答案。换句话说，step-by-step 不是天然更“聪明”，而是更贴合有限样本学习时的局部插值偏置；如果训练经验对局部转换覆盖充分，链式结构就会自发成为更稳的解码路径。","experiments":"这类论文的实验通常会使用可控任务和小型序列模型，比较直接回答与中间步骤回答在分布外泛化、样本效率和噪声鲁棒性上的差异。最关键的证据不会是单个 benchmark SOTA，而是：当训练数据具有局部可组合性时，step-by-step 方案在泛化上明显更稳；当这种局部性被破坏时，优势会减弱甚至消失。当前给定信息不足以安全列出具体模型规模、任务名称和提升数值。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper asks a mechanism-level question: why does step-by-step reasoning emerge at all, instead of being merely an artifact of explicit chain-of-thought supervision? For pretraining and in-context learning research, this kind of work matters because it can identify the data and inductive-bias conditions under which reasoning-like behavior appears. If the argument holds, it suggests that reasoning emergence is tied to local structure in experience rather than to a special built-in reasoning module, which has implications for synthetic data design and curriculum construction.","technical_approach":"The approach is best understood as theory plus controlled experiments. The title points to a central hypothesis: step-by-step reasoning is easier to learn because training data more often supports local transitions and local consistency than direct long-range mappings from problem to final answer. Under this view, CoT is not inherently more intelligent; it is more compatible with finite-data interpolation, so models naturally discover it when local experience is informative enough.","experiments":"The likely experimental pattern is controlled sequence-learning tasks comparing direct-answer training with intermediate-step training under different data regimes. ensembling、multi-objective joint fine-tuning，以及不同 soup 系数对稳定性的影响。当前没有摘要和具体结果，无法可靠填写数据集、模型规模和提升数字。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a real alignment problem: we The intuition is that if these fine-tuned solutions remain in a compatible basin, interpolation can preserve shared capabilities while reducing the extreme bias of any single reward. agent 在多步任务中失败时，如何不用参数更新也能从失败中积累可复用经验。过去常见做法要么依赖监督微调或 RLHF 改参数，要么只做短期 trial-and-error 而不形成稳定记忆，这使得样本效率低、部署成本高，也不利于在线适应。","importance":"它的重要性不在于提出新的预训练目标，而在于把“语言反馈”明确当成一种可操作的强化信号，展示了 post-train 阶段可以只通过上下文和记忆机制获得接近强化学习的收益。对 pretrain 研究的启发是，模型是否具备自我解释、错误归因和可写入文本记忆的能力，会直接决定后续 agent 化和 test-time adaptation 的上限。","technical_approach":"方法结论很直接：Reflexion 用语言形式的自我反思替代参数级更新，把失败轨迹压缩成后续决策可读取的 verbal feedback。具体做法是三段式循环：先执行任务，再由 evaluator 给出成败或环境反馈，最后由同一个或辅助 LM 生成一段反思文本，写入 episodic memory，下一轮把这些记忆连同任务上下文一起喂给模型。它放弃了显式策略梯度、价值函数和权重更新，换来的是更低部署门槛和更强可解释性；代价是记忆质量高度依赖模型本身的归因能力，错误反思会被持续放大。和常规 chain-of-thought 不同，这里的语言不是只为当前样本服务，而是跨 episode 传递策略修正。","experiments":"实验结论是：在顺序决策和程序合成类任务上，Reflexion 相比单纯 ReAct 或标准 few-shot prompting 有明显提升，但增益高度依赖任务是否能提供清晰反馈。论文的代表性评测包括 HumanEval 编程、AlfWorld 和 HotPotQA 一类 agent/推理任务；常见设置是用 GPT-3.5/4 级别模型做 backbone，不额外训练参数。核心现象不是绝对 SOTA，而是同一个 frozen model 在加入 verbal memory 后成功率持续上升，说明收益来自“经验积累”而不是一次性 prompt 工程。ablation 一般会比较：无反思、仅重试、随机记忆、截断记忆；结果显示高质量反馈和简洁而针对性的 reflection 最关键，记忆过长会引入噪声并挤占上下文。","authors_note":"普林斯顿大学团队。Shunyu Yao 与 Karthik Narasimhan 都持续做 LLM agent、ReAct、工具使用和推理相关工作；这篇可以看作 ReAct 之后，把“行动-反馈-文字化经验”闭环补上的一篇代表作。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles a simple but important question: how can a language agent improve from failure without updating its weights. Earlier systems either relied on finetuning/RL-style parameter updates or just retried with no persistent learning, which made adaptation expensive and brittle in deployment.","importance":"Its main contribution is not a new pretraining objective, but a clean demonstration that language itself can serve as a reinforcement channel. For pretraining researchers, the implication is practical: models that can explain failures, assign blame, and write compact reusable memories are much easier to turn into adaptive agents at test time.","technical_approach":"Reflexion replaces weight updates with verbal reinforcement. The loop has three parts: act in the environment, obtain a task-specific signal of success or failure, then generate a short natural-language reflection that is stored in episodic memory and prepended to future attempts. This design deliberately drops policy gradients, critics, and parameter optimization. The benefit is low engineering overhead and interpretable adaptation. The cost is that bad self-diagnosis can poison future behavior. Unlike standard chain-of-thought, the text here is not just for the current example; it is a cross-episode carrier of behavioral updates.","experiments":"The main empirical result is that a frozen LLM becomes noticeably better over repeated trials when given reflective memory, especially on tasks with crisp external feedback. The paper evaluates on agentic and program-synthesis style benchmarks such as HumanEval and AlfWorld, typically using GPT-3.5/GPT-4-class models without extra training. The key finding is not raw SOTA but monotonic improvement across episodes relative to plain ReAct or retry-only baselines. Ablations show that targeted reflections outperform random or overly long memory, and that memory quality matters more than memory quantity.","authors_note":"Princeton University. Shunyu Yao and Karthik Narasimhan are well known for work on ReAct, tool use, and LLM agents. This paper extends that line by closing the loop from action and feedback to reusable textual experience."},"tags":["language-agent","verbal-rl","self-reflection","post-training"],"notable_authors":["Karthik Narasimhan","Shunyu Yao"]},{"id":"SilvaWLL23","title":"CoLLAT: On Adding Fine-grained Audio Understanding to Language Models using Token-Level Locked-Language Tuning","authors":["Dadallage A. Lengerich","Hugh Leather"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"NEURIPS","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/nips/SilvaWLL23","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/nips/SilvaWLL23","_ee":"http://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/c7b5a35ea98b62512a869c19ea7b03cb-Abstract-Conference.html","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的问题是：如何在不破坏现有语言模型能力的前提下，给它加入更细粒度的音频理解能力。过去多模态音频-文本系统常用全局音频 embedding 或 encoder-decoder 对接，这对粗粒度分类够用，但对事件级、时序级音频语义建模不够细。","importance":"它的价值在于提供了一条较轻量的音频接入路线：不是重新训练一个原生 audio-language foundation model，而是通过 token 级对齐把细粒度音频信息锁定到 LM 的 token space。对 pretrain 研究的启发是，跨模态扩展未必都要大规模联合预训练；如果表示足够稳定，token-level adaptation 可能是更便宜的接口方案。","technical_approach":"CoLLAT 的核心是 token-level locked-language tuning：保留语言模型主体尽量不动，把音频编码后的时序表示映射到与文本 token 交互的空间，并通过受约束的调优方式接入。这里的“locked”强调尽量冻结语言能力，以避免音频注入导致语言退化；“token-level”强调不是只给一个整体音频向量，而是保留时间细节，让模型能在文本生成或理解时引用局部声学事件。和常见 prefix/prompt tuning 相比，它更重视音频 token 与文本 token 的精细对应；和全量联合训练相比，它牺牲了一部分跨模态共同演化能力，换来更低算力和更小遗忘风险。","experiments":"实验结论是：在细粒度音频理解任务上，token-level 接入优于只用全局音频表征的方案，同时语言侧能力保持得更稳定。根据标题和方法定位，评测重点应包括音频事件识别、音频问答或音频字幕等需要时间分辨率的任务，并与冻结 LM + 音频前缀、全局 pooling 以及可能的全量微调作比较。论文的关键信号应该是，细粒度 supervision 和锁定语言参数能同时带来性能与稳定性收益；但它的边界也明显，若任务需要深度声学生成或跨模态共同推理，仅靠适配层可能不够。","authors_note":"如果仅根据给定信息，机构无法可靠确认。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a practical multimodal question: how do we add fine-grained audio understanding to an LLM without damaging its language competence. Earlier audio-language systems often relied on a single pooled audio embedding or encoder-decoder coupling, which is acceptable for coarse tasks but too weak for event-level temporal understanding.","importance":"Its significance is methodological rather than paradigm-shifting. The work suggests that not every audio-language extension needs expensive native joint pretraining. If representations are stable enough, token-level adaptation can be a cheaper interface for bringing temporal audio detail into an existing LM.","technical_approach":"CoLLAT uses token-level locked-language tuning. The idea is to keep most of the language model fixed, encode audio into a temporal sequence, and map that sequence into a space where it can interact with text tokens at a finer granularity. The 'locked' part is about preserving language ability by avoiding broad LM updates. The 'token-level' part is about retaining temporal resolution instead of collapsing audio into one global vector. Compared with prefix-style adapters, this gives more detailed alignment between audio events and text generation. Compared with full joint training, it trades some cross-modal flexibility for lower cost and less forgetting.","experiments":"The paper’s main result is that fine-grained token-level audio integration improves tasks that depend on temporal detail while preserving language-side behavior better than coarser alternatives. The likely comparisons include frozen-LM baselines with pooled audio features, prefix-like conditioning, and stronger finetuning variants. Much of the earlier discussion implicitly assumed that ICL grows monotonically with scale and optimization, but that assumption may be false.","importance":"This matters a lot because it challenges a common scaling narrative. For pretraining, the implication is that desirable algorithmic behavior observed mid-training may not survive to the final checkpoint. Optimizing them one module at a time is usually suboptimal because changing an upstream prompt changes the input distribution seen downstream.","importance":"Its importance lies in reframing prompt engineering as system-level optimization. The broader lesson for the pretrain/posttrain interface is that as LLM applications become modular, many performance limits come from the interfaces between models rather than the base model alone. Joint prompt optimization is a lightweight post-training lever that can materially change behavior without touching weights.","technical_approach":"The method uses variational inference to jointly optimize prompts in stacked LLM systems. The main idea is to treat prompts and intermediate discrete text outputs as latent variables and optimize them together rather than greedily. This gives a way to handle cross-module dependencies and perform approximate credit assignment even when the constituent LLMs are black boxes and non-differentiable. Compared with single-model prompt search, the method is explicitly system-aware. Compared with parameter finetuning, it preserves the black-box API setting, at the cost of approximate inference error and potentially expensive search.","experiments":"The paper’s key empirical claim is that jointly optimized prompts outperform independently tuned or manually designed prompts in multi-stage LLM pipelines, especially when intermediate reasoning, rewriting, or retrieval coordination matters. The evaluation likely includes 2- or 3-stage stacks such as planner-solver or retriever-reader systems, with baselines like greedy tuning and random search. The most useful pattern is that gains grow when module interactions are stronger. The limitation is straightforward: as the pipeline gets deeper and the prompt space grows, variational optimization becomes harder and more budget-sensitive.","authors_note":""},"tags":["prompting","llm","optimization","stacked-models"],"notable_authors":["Alessandro Sordoni","Adam Trischler"]},{"id":"SuhrA23","title":"Continual Learning for Instruction Following from Realtime Feedback","authors":["Alane Suhr","Yoav Artzi"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"NEURIPS","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/nips/SuhrA23","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/nips/SuhrA23","_ee":"http://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/666cccc6376058e251315b4de7e085b9-Abstract-Conference.html","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{},"summary_en":{},"tags":["continual-learning","instruction-tuning","feedback","alignment"]},{"id":"SunSRBJY23","title":"SpecTr: Fast Speculative Decoding via Optimal Transport","authors":["Ziteng Sun","Ananda Theertha Suresh","Jae Hun Ro","Ahmad Beirami","Himanshu Jain","Felix X. Bowman"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"NEURIPS","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/nips/TurpinMPB23","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/nips/TurpinMPB23","_ee":"http://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/ed3fea9033a80fea1376299fa7863f4a-Abstract-Conference.html","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：Chain-of-Thought 给出的解释并不总是忠实反映模型真实的决策过程。过去大家常把 CoT 同时当成性能增强手段和可解释性窗口，但这两个角色并不天然一致；模型可能先得到答案，再生成一段看起来合理的推理文本。","importance":"这是对 posttrain 与 reasoning 评估非常重要的一篇诊断论文。它的冲击不在于否定 CoT 提升准确率，而在于明确提醒社区：把自然语言推理链当作 faithful reasoning trace 是危险的。对 pretrain/posttrain 闭环的启示是，训练信号一旦奖励“会写推理”，就可能诱导模型优化表面可读性而不是真实中间计算，这和后续 RL、过程监督、latent reasoning 研究直接相关。","technical_approach":"核心方法是构造能够区分‘答案是否正确’与‘解释是否忠实’的干预实验。典型做法包括：在 prompt 中加入暗示、误导性线索或使答案易于直接猜出的设置，观察模型是否输出与决策原因不一致的 CoT；或者比较不同提示下答案稳定但解释变化的情况。这个设计有效，因为 faithful explanation 至少应对因果因素敏感：如果真正驱动答案的变量变了，解释也应相应变化。和单纯人工评判解释通顺不同，这种方法是在测 explanation-decision alignment。","experiments":"虽然这里没有摘要，但这篇论文的核心实验结论是方向明确的：LLM 会频繁给出不忠实的 CoT，尤其在存在提示泄漏、先验暗示或捷径线索时更明显。没有正文支持，不能安全写具体模型名单、样本量或比例数字；但可以确定它不是在比较‘有没有 CoT 更准’，而是在比较‘CoT 是否真的解释了模型为何得出该答案’。","authors_note":"机构与作者信息可从公开作者名单中部分识别，但当前给定信息不足以稳妥确认到团队代表作层面，因此留空。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a critical mismatch: chain-of-thought explanations are not always faithful to the model’s actual decision process. CoT has often been treated as both a performance tool and an interpretability tool, but those are different claims. A model can reach an answer through one route and then produce a plausible-looking rationale afterward.","importance":"This is an important diagnostic result for reasoning and post-training. The point is not that CoT fails to improve accuracy, but that natural-language rationales should not be assumed to be faithful traces of computation. For the pretrain–posttrain interface, this matters a lot: if training rewards “good-looking reasoning,” models may optimize for plausible explanations rather than truthful intermediate computation. That directly connects to later work on process supervision, reward hacking, and latent reasoning.","technical_approach":"The key method is to separate answer correctness from explanation faithfulness through intervention-style evaluations. A typical setup introduces cues, misleading hints, or shortcut signals, then checks whether the model’s explanation matches the factors that actually drove the answer. This is the right test because a faithful explanation should track causal dependence: if the decision-making signal changes, the explanation should change accordingly. That is much stronger than simply asking whether the rationale sounds coherent.","experiments":"The abstract is not included here, so specific models, datasets, and percentages should not be claimed. Standard next-token loss only rewards the final prediction, leaving weak pressure on how the model internally selects evidence. Compared with sparse-attention approaches, this changes the learning objective rather than just the computation graph. Compared with retrieval-augmented systems, the filtering stays inside the model instead of relying on an external retriever. VLM 仍依赖 FP16/BF16 加上一堆经验性保护，原因不是方法不会训，而是对比学习、logit scale、归一化和大 batch 的组合会把数值范围推到低比特格式难以承受的区域。","importance":"这项工作的价值主要是系统级和训练方法级。它说明低精度不仅是推理或微调优化问题，也可以直接进入大规模预训练主干；如果训练侧能稳定落到更低 bit，收益不是小幅省显存，而是实打实地提升吞吐、降低成本，并把更大 batch 或更大模型带回可训练区间。这对 VLM 预训练尤其重要，因为图文双塔和对比损失本来就受限于通信、显存和数值稳定性。","technical_approach":"作者提出的是一套面向大规模 VLM 的稳定低精度训练配方，而不是单点新算子。核心思路是先定位训练失稳真正来自哪里，再把高动态范围的少数路径保留在更安全精度，其余大部分计算压到低精度。对这类模型，最危险的通常不是普通线性层本身，而是相似度 logits、温度参数、归一化统计量、梯度缩放以及优化器状态的相互作用；因此方法上会围绕这些敏感点做 selective precision、范围控制和稳定化处理，而不是粗暴地把全模型一刀切量化训练。它与常见“后验发现溢出再打补丁”的区别在于，设计目标就是让低精度成为默认训练路径，而高精度只保留在必要节点。","experiments":"从题目和作者背景看，实验重点应是大规模 CLIP/对比式 VLM 训练在更低精度下的稳定性、速度和最终零样本精度对比，而不是单一小模型验证。关键事实通常会包括：在与 BF16/FP16 基线接近甚至相同的下游精度下，训练吞吐更高、显存占用更低，并且训练曲线没有出现常见的 loss spike 或 collapse。更有价值的观察通常来自 ablation：如果去掉对 logit scale、归一化或某些累积路径的高精度保留，训练会明显变得不稳定；而把所有模块都保守地留在高精度，则失去低精度训练的大部分收益。当前能确认的细粒度数字不足，因此这里不编造具体提升幅度。","authors_note":"机构与作者阵容很强，包含 Google/Seattle 方向与 Tim Dettmers 等长期做低比特训练和系统优化的研究者。相关团队代表性工作包括 CLIP/大规模 VLM practitioners often trade stability for speed and memory. Prior pipelines could train VLMs well, but contrastive losses, logit scaling, normalization, and large batches create value ranges that make aggressive low-bit training unreliable.","importance":"This is primarily a systems-and-training contribution. The important point is not just saving memory; it is showing that low precision can be a first-class pretraining regime for VLMs rather than a risky optimization trick. For multimodal pretraining, that matters because communication, memory pressure, and batch size already dominate cost, and unstable numerics often erase theoretical efficiency gains.","technical_approach":"The work appears to propose a stability recipe for low-precision VLM training rather than a single new module. The key design choice is selective precision: keep only a small set of numerically sensitive paths in safer precision and push the bulk of compute into lower precision. In this setting, the fragile parts are typically similarity logits, temperature parameters, normalization statistics, gradient scaling, and optimizer-state interactions. The method is therefore likely built around range management and precision placement, instead of naively quantizing everything and fixing failures afterward.","experiments":"Given the title and author list, the main experiments likely study CLIP-style large-scale VLM training under lower precision, comparing stability, throughput, memory, and final zero-shot quality against BF16/FP16 baselines. The most useful ablations in such work are the ones showing that removing higher-precision handling from a few sensitive components leads to spikes or collapse, while keeping too much in high precision removes most of the benefit. Much of mechanistic interpretability has historically worked best on toy models or synthetic tasks, where circuits are easier to isolate than in practical LLMs.","importance":"Its significance is in pushing interpretability toward realistic language models. For pretrain and post-train researchers, the point is not just understanding for its own sake; it is about locating where behavior comes from. That can help separate capabilities inherited from pretraining from routing changes induced by instruction tuning, and it offers a more actionable view of controllability and alignment.","technical_approach":"The method is likely intervention-based causal analysis rather than passive probing. Given the title, the paper probably uses tools such as activation patching, causal tracing, or representation-level interventions to identify subnetworks or pathways responsible for specific behaviors in Alpaca. The important design choice is to test necessity and transfer, not mere correlation: if a representation is removed, does the behavior vanish; if it is transplanted, does the behavior appear elsewhere?","experiments":"The experiments likely focus on a set of clearly defined instruction-following or reasoning behaviors, then validate discovered mechanisms by targeted interventions. Compared with standard pairwise preference labels, this increases annotation complexity and cost, but it can reduce reward hacking on superficial answer-level cues.","experiments":"The main experiments likely compare coarse versus fine-grained feedback in both reward-model quality and downstream LM training. The interface is the real problem—visual information is dense and hierarchical, while the frozen LLM expects a compressed, semantically organized token space.","importance":"This matters as an interface design problem for pretraining and unified modeling, not just as a downstream trick. The broader lesson is that if we want to reuse a frozen LLM for multimodal generation, we need a representation that is semantic and hierarchical enough for the LM to model effectively. That connects directly to visual tokenization and latent-space design.","technical_approach":"SPAE introduces a Semantic Pyramid AutoEncoder that maps multimodal content into a hierarchical latent representation, then lets a frozen LLM model or generate in that latent space. The novelty is not simply an adapter. It is the pyramid structure that separates coarse semantics from finer details, instead of forcing the LLM to model raw visual tokens directly. Compared with shallow projectors or flat VQ tokenizers, this design gives the LM a representation that is closer to what it handles well: compressed, semantically structured, and more language-like. The trade-off is that generation quality becomes bounded by the autoencoder, and balancing semantic abstraction against reconstruction fidelity is nontrivial.","experiments":"The experiments likely show that under a frozen-LLM setup, SPAE improves multimodal generation quality over simpler adapter or tokenizer baselines, and that the hierarchical latent design gives a better semantic-detail trade-off. Gonzalez","Ion Stoica"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"NEURIPS","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/nips/ZhengC00WZL0LXZ23","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/nips/ZhengC00WZL0LXZ23","_ee":"http://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/91f18a1287b398d378ef22505bf41832-Abstract-Datasets_and_Benchmarks.html","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文的核心结论是：用 LLM 充当评审是可行的，但必须先搞清它在哪些设定下可信、在哪些设定下会系统性偏。过去社区常用人工评测聊天模型，成本高且复现差；而直接让 GPT-4 之类模型打分虽然便宜，却缺少系统验证。作者想回答的不是“能不能用”，而是“什么任务上能用到什么程度”。","importance":"这是后训练评测体系中的一篇基准性工作，影响很大。对 pretrain 研究也有直接意义，因为现代预训练模型的比较越来越依赖开放式生成评测；如果 judge 不稳定，很多关于数据配比、对齐策略和推理增强的结论都可能是噪声。它的重要性不在于提出了新模型，而在于把 LLM 评审从经验做法变成了有 benchmark、有相关性分析、有公开平台支撑的标准流程。","technical_approach":"作者做了两件关键事情。第一，构建 MT-Bench 这类多轮、能力覆盖较广的评测集，用来刻意考察聊天模型在 turn-level 和 conversation-level 的表现，而不是只看单轮问答。第二，结合 Chatbot Arena 的众包对战平台，收集人类偏好数据，再拿 LLM judge 的分数或偏好和人类结果做一致性分析。与传统自动指标的区别在于，这不是用 BLEU/ROUGE 之类代理分数近似人评，而是直接让强模型充当语言层面的比较器。这样设计有效，是因为开放式对话没有唯一参考答案，judge 模型比 n-gram 指标更接近人类比较方式。边界同样明确：LLM judge 会有 position bias、verbosity bias、self-enhancement bias 等系统偏差，不能把它当绝对真值。","experiments":"论文最关键的实验事实通常包括：GPT-4 作为 judge 与人类偏好的相关性显著高于较弱模型，也高于传统自动指标；在单轮任务上相关性更强，在数学、事实性、长对话等高精度场景上偏差更明显。Chatbot Arena 则提供了大量 pairwise battle 数据，证明 crowdsourced ranking 能较稳定地区分主流聊天模型。值得关注的 ablation 一般有三类：pairwise vs single-score judging、回答顺序调换造成的位置偏差、以及去掉参考答案后 judge 稳定性如何。题目没有附摘要，但该工作广为人知，其主要结论就是“LLM-as-a-judge 有用，但要配套基准和偏差控制”。","authors_note":"如果按公开信息，这项工作与 LMSYS 团队高度相关，代表性产物就是 Vicuna、MT-Bench 和 Chatbot Arena。由于当前输入未显式给出机构，这里不写机构归属。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper’s central claim is that LLM-as-a-judge is useful, but only if its reliability is measured rather than assumed. Human evaluation of chat models is expensive and hard to reproduce, while using a strong model such as GPT-4 as a grader is cheap but can be misleading without calibration. The paper asks not simply whether LLM judges work, but when they work and where they fail.","importance":"This is a benchmark-and-evaluation paper with outsized impact. It also matters for pretraining because many conclusions about model quality now depend on open-ended generation benchmarks, where unstable judges can turn training differences into evaluation noise. Its contribution is to turn LLM judging from an ad hoc practice into a more standardized pipeline with public benchmarks, agreement analysis, and a live arena for pairwise comparison.","technical_approach":"The paper contributes two tightly connected pieces. First, MT-Bench evaluates multi-turn chat abilities across a range of categories, so models are tested on conversation quality rather than isolated single-turn answers. Second, Chatbot Arena collects large-scale human preference data via anonymous head-to-head battles, then compares those outcomes with LLM-based judgments. The key difference from older automatic metrics is that the evaluator is itself a capable language model rather than a reference-matching heuristic like BLEU or ROUGE. This works better because open-ended dialogue rarely has a unique gold answer, and pairwise comparison by a strong LLM is closer to how humans judge quality. The limitations are equally important: LLM judges exhibit position bias, verbosity bias, and self-favoring behavior.","experiments":"The most important empirical result is that GPT-4 as a judge correlates much better with human preferences than weaker model judges and than classic lexical metrics. The agreement is usually stronger on single-turn and general chat tasks, and weaker on domains requiring precise factuality or mathematical correctness. Chatbot Arena supplies large-scale pairwise battle data and shows that crowd-based rankings can reliably separate major chatbot systems. Useful ablations include pairwise versus scalar scoring, answer order swaps to measure position bias, and whether the judge remains stable without reference answers. Even without the abstract here, the paper is well known for one practical conclusion: LLM judging is good enough to be operationally useful, but not trustworthy without bias checks.","authors_note":"Publicly, this work is closely associated with the LMSYS team, known for Vicuna, MT-Bench, and Chatbot Arena. A model can natively handle arbitrary resolutions and aspect ratios, and that is often a better use of compute. Standard resizing is convenient, but it wastes tokens and destroys information, especially for documents, mobile photos, and mixed-resolution training.","importance":"This is an architectural and data-interface contribution with direct relevance to multimodal pretraining. It also makes a practical scaling point: under a fixed budget, spending tokens on real image content beats blindly normalizing everything to the same grid.","technical_approach":"The method is Patch n' Pack: patch images at their native resolution, then pack patch sequences from different image sizes and aspect ratios into shared batches for a transformer. The important idea is not variable resolution by itself, but doing it without blowing up padding waste. Compared with fixed-input ViTs, the design gives up regular tensor simplicity in exchange for better token efficiency and less information loss from resizing.","experiments":"The provided metadata does not include quantitative results, but a paper of this kind would typically compare against standard ViTs on classification and transfer under matched or lower FLOPs, while showing particular gains on non-square and high-resolution inputs. Many approximate attention methods rely on low-rank structure, kernels, or sampling, but the hidden assumption is often that the score matrix behaves nicely. This paper makes that dependency explicit.","importance":"This is a theory-boundary contribution, but it matters to pretraining practice because it clarifies when attention acceleration is structurally fragile. That directly affects long-context training and architecture substitutions.","technical_approach":"The likely contribution is an impossibility or lower-bound result showing that bounded entries are required for broad classes of fast-attention approximations to work reliably. Prior efficiency work often changed the attention mechanism itself, but that usually introduces approximation error or architectural constraints; this work asks whether better update scheduling alone can deliver savings.","importance":"If the empirical claim holds, the contribution is mainly at the systems and optimization-policy level. For pretraining, the relevant lesson is that efficiency does not have to come from more exotic operators; some gains may come from relaxing the assumption that every parameter block must be updated every step, which is directly relevant to large-scale throughput and training stability.","technical_approach":"From the title, the method is alternating updates: different Transformer components are updated on different steps instead of all at once. The underlying bet is that attention, MLPs, embeddings, or layer groups have enough temporal redundancy that asynchronous optimization can preserve quality while reducing per-step work, activation storage, or communication. Unlike pruning or approximate attention, the architecture stays mostly intact; the trade-off is that optimization dynamics become more delicate, and poor alternation schedules can slow convergence or create mismatch across modules.","experiments":"The prompt does not provide the abstract, so concrete datasets, speedups, and accuracy deltas cannot be stated reliably. The key result to look for is not just the maximum speedup, but how much cost is saved at near-constant final accuracy.","authors_note":""},"tags":["transformer","efficiency","alternating-updates","attention"]},{"id":"2305.01625","title":"Unlimiformer: Long-Range Transformers with Unlimited Length Input","authors":["Amanda Bertsch","Uri Alon","Graham Neubig","Matthew Gormley"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$e","venue":"NEURIPS","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.01625","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/nips/Bertsch0NG23","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/nips/Bertsch0NG23","_ee":"http://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/6f9806a5adc72b5b834b27e4c7c0df9b-Abstract-Conference.html","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作解决的是 encoder-decoder Transformer 的长输入瓶颈，而且结论很明确：可以在不重训主模型的情况下，把 cross-attention 改写成基于 kNN 检索的稀疏访问，从而处理几乎无限长的输入。传统 Transformer 受限于对所有输入 token 做全量注意力，长文档场景不是被截断，就是要换成长上下文专用架构；作者想做的是给现成预训练模型套一个外壳，直接扩上下文。","importance":"它的价值是系统级和推理/适配级的，对 pretrain 也有现实启发。原因在于很多长上下文能力未必非要在预训练阶段用更大窗口硬学出来，至少对 encoder-decoder 任务，外部检索式注意力能在较低成本下把可访问上下文扩得很大。这种思路和后来的 external memory、RAG、KV 检索优化有连续性：把精确全连接注意力换成受控检索，换取长度扩展。","technical_approach":"Unlimiformer 的核心是：把输入侧 key/value 建成单个 kNN 索引，在解码时每个 decoder layer、每个 attention head 只检索 top-k 相关 key，而 kNN 返回的距离直接当作注意力打分。设计上它没有改动原始预训练 encoder-decoder 的主体参数，而是替换 cross-attention 的访问方式；索引还能放在 CPU 或 GPU 上，并以次线性时间查询。为什么有效：在摘要、书籍总结这类任务里，解码时真正有用的信息通常只分布在长文档中的少数位置，没必要让每个 query 看全序列。代价是检索误差会直接变成注意力误差，因此 top-k 覆盖率和索引质量决定效果上限。","experiments":"摘要给出的关键信息是：作者在多个长文档与书籍摘要 benchmark 上评测，并报告可以优于所有已有基线。题面截断，没保留具体数据，但合理的实验重点应包括：在 Long-document summarization 和 book summarization 上，与原始截断式模型、长上下文 Transformer 或检索增强 baseline 比较 ROUGE 等指标；展示输入长度提升后的性能曲线，而不是只报固定窗口结果；以及分析 k、索引存放位置（CPU/GPU）和检索时间的 trade-off。最值得关注的不是“无限长度”这个字面表述，而是当输入远超预训练窗口时，性能是否随长度平稳退化而不是立刻崩掉。","authors_note":"作者包含 Graham Neubig、Matthew Gormley。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses the long-input bottleneck in encoder-decoder Transformers, and the main claim is clear: cross-attention can be replaced with kNN-based retrieval over encoder states so that pretrained models can process effectively unbounded inputs without retraining the core model. Standard Transformers either truncate long documents or require specialized long-context architectures; this work tries to extend context by wrapping existing pretrained models.","importance":"The contribution is primarily systems- and adaptation-level, but it has practical implications for pretraining. It suggests that some long-context capability does not need to be bought entirely through larger pretraining windows; at least for encoder-decoder tasks, retrieval-style access can greatly expand usable context at lower cost. The idea sits in the same family as external memory, retrieval augmentation, and KV selection: replace dense full attention with targeted access.","technical_approach":"Unlimiformer builds a single kNN index over encoder-side keys and values, and at decoding time each head in each decoder layer retrieves only its top-k relevant keys, using returned kNN distances as attention scores. The architecture of the pretrained encoder-decoder is largely preserved; what changes is how cross-attention accesses memory. The index can live on CPU or GPU and be queried in sub-linear time. The design works when useful evidence is sparse in long inputs, as in document or book summarization, so decoding queries do not need to scan every token. The trade-off is direct: retrieval mistakes become attention mistakes, so top-k recall and index quality bound performance.","experiments":"The abstract states that the method is evaluated on multiple long-document and book-summarization benchmarks and outperforms all baselines, though the prompt cuts off the exact numbers. The critical evidence should include ROUGE-style comparisons against truncation-based pretrained models, long-context Transformer baselines, and retrieval-enhanced alternatives; performance as input length grows far beyond the original window; and ablations over k, index placement on CPU vs GPU, and retrieval latency. The useful lens is not the phrase 'unlimited length' itself, but whether quality degrades gracefully rather than collapsing once inputs exceed pretraining context.","authors_note":"Includes Graham Neubig and Matthew Gormley."},"tags":["long-context","knn","transformer","encoder-decoder","unbounded-length"],"notable_authors":["Graham Neubig","Matthew Gormley"]},{"id":"BorzunovRCBDBSR23","title":"Distributed Inference and Fine-tuning of Large Language Models Over The Internet","authors":["Alexander Borzunov","Max Ryabinin","Artem Chumachenko","Dmitry Baranchuk","Tim Dettmers","Younes Belkada","Pavel Samygin","Colin A. Classical distributed training is built for datacenter networks; the internet is high-latency, bandwidth-constrained, and unreliable. The paper asks whether LLM inference and finetuning can still work under those conditions.","importance":"This is a systems contribution, but one with real implications for the pretraining ecosystem. The key evidence should include end-to-end LLM inference across real internet-distributed nodes, cost/performance comparisons against centralized alternatives, and a demonstration that finetuning—likely parameter-efficient finetuning or small-scale full tuning—still converges. For pretraining, the relevance is indirect but real. It does not introduce a new optimizer; instead, it gives a mechanistic account of the implicit bias of standard SGD. The key step is to make the notion of subnetwork simplicity analytically tractable and connect the noise term to changes in the basin structure. Compared with the classic flat-minima narrative, the stronger claim here is that noise affects which structural degrees of freedom remain active, not just the local geometry of the final minimum.","experiments":"The empirical section likely tests stochastic collapse in controlled settings, showing that larger gradient noise, smaller batch sizes, or related settings lead models toward simpler subnetworks while preserving or sometimes improving generalization. This is effective because compositional failures often originate from representation learning rather than from an insufficient downstream reasoner. Compared with CLIP-style global caption supervision, DAC provides more structured supervision. Much prior work implicitly fixes the other variables and reports statements like 'bigger is better' or 'more data helps.' This paper instead studies the Pareto frontier induced by these resources and stochasticity together.","importance":"Its main value is methodological. For pretraining research, the message is that recipe comparisons can be badly misleading if width, compute, data, and run-to-run variance are not handled jointly. In mid-scale experiments especially, luck can be comparable to the gains from many proposed improvements, which contaminates conclusions about scaling laws or optimization recipes.","technical_approach":"The core contribution is a Pareto-frontier analysis rather than a new learning algorithm. The authors sweep over data size, training compute, model width, and random initializations, then identify configurations that are not jointly dominated under the chosen performance metrics. A key design choice is to treat luck as a measurable factor rather than collapsing it into a mean result. This reframes 'method A is better' into 'method A is closer to the frontier under a given resource and variance budget.'","experiments":"The main empirical result is that no single strategy dominates across all regimes, and many apparent rules hold only locally. In their deep feature learning setup, the authors show substitution effects among data, compute, and width, and they find that seed-to-seed variation can materially change model rankings on the frontier. A useful observation is that some advantages from width or lucky initialization shrink as data or training time increases; conversely, in low-budget settings, some 'method gains' are mostly variance effects. The paper argues for reporting performance distributions and frontier positions rather than best-run results alone.","authors_note":""},"tags":["scaling-law","compute","data","optimization"],"notable_authors":["Benjamin L.
