These events are usually attributed loosely to large learning rates or exploding gradients, but that view does not explain the abrupt transitions well.","importance":"Its contribution is a mechanism-level account of training instability rather than a stronger sequence model. For pretraining, the useful lesson is that loss spikes may mark crossings of stability boundaries in the training dynamics, not merely engineering mishaps. That perspective is relevant to edge-of-stability behavior, learning-rate schedules, and fragile long-horizon training regimes more broadly.","technical_approach":"The paper analyzes RNN training through bifurcation theory. It models training as a discrete dynamical system and studies how the stability of fixed points changes with hyperparameters and parameter state, explaining why loss can jump sharply in certain regions. Unlike diagnostics that only track gradient norms or top Hessian eigenvalues, this approach focuses on qualitative changes in the phase portrait: when training leaves one stable basin for another dynamical regime, degradation can be abrupt rather than gradual.","experiments":"The main empirical result is that controlled RNN training runs exhibit loss jumps that align with predicted bifurcation points, suggesting the effect is systematic rather than random. The authors vary learning rates, initialization, and task settings and show repeated transitions into unstable behavior near critical regions. The useful part is reproducibility and controllability: these jumps can be shifted or avoided by changing optimization settings. The paper is more about validating a dynamical explanation than about benchmark performance.","authors_note":""},"tags":["training-dynamics","loss-spikes","rnn"],"notable_authors":["Lukas Eisenmann","Zahra Monfared","Niclas Alexander Göring","Daniel Durstewitz"]},{"id":"EschenhagenIT0H23","title":"Kronecker-Factored Approximate Curvature for Modern Neural Network Architectures","authors":["Runa Eschenhagen","Alexander Immer","Richard E. Turner","Frank Schneider","Philipp Hennig"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"NEURIPS","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/nips/EschenhagenIT0H23","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/nips/EschenhagenIT0H23","_ee":"http://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/6a6679e3d5b9f7d5f09cdb79a5fc3fd8-Abstract-Conference.html","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的核心问题是：经典 K-FAC 在现代网络架构上适配不够好，导致二阶近似虽然理论吸引人，但在 Transformer、归一化层、参数共享等现实组件上落地困难。过去很多工作要么停留在卷积/MLP，要么为了适配现代架构做很多特化近似；作者希望给出更统一、现代化的 K-FAC 处理方式。","importance":"它的重要性在于把近似二阶优化从“理论上有用”往“现代模型可用”推进了一步，属于优化系统与方法接口层面的贡献。对 pretrain 的启示是，大模型训练长期依赖 AdamW 这类一阶方法，并不代表曲率信息没价值；真正障碍往往在工程适配、内存通信和不规则模块支持。若 K-FAC 能更稳地覆盖现代架构，它在大 batch、少步数训练或后期收敛阶段可能重新变得有吸引力。","technical_approach":"核心方法是对 K-FAC 做面向现代架构的泛化与实现改造。作者重新整理了不同层类型的 Kronecker 分解近似方式，使其能处理更广泛的模块组合，并给出在自动微分框架中更统一的曲率因子构造。关键区别不是发明新的二阶理论，而是降低 K-FAC 与现实模型之间的错配：哪些层可以安全共享近似、哪些统计量需要稳定估计、哪些模块需要额外修正。设计目标是在保持 K-FAC 结构优势的同时减少手工特例。","experiments":"实验结论是：改造后的 K-FAC 能在一系列现代架构上稳定运行，并在若干设置下比一阶方法更具样本效率或步数效率。论文通常比较 SGD/Adam 与 K-FAC 变体，在训练速度、最终精度和 wall-clock 代价之间做权衡。更值得关注的是工程侧结果：如果曲率估计更新过频，成本会迅速上升；若更新过稀，则收益下降。作者的实现和消融表明，适中的因子更新频率、阻尼与近似选择决定了 K-FAC 是否真正实用。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a practical limitation of classical K-FAC: it does not transfer cleanly to many modern architectures, so curvature-based optimization remains appealing in theory but awkward in current models. Earlier work often focused on MLPs and convnets or relied on many architecture-specific approximations. This paper aims to make K-FAC more compatible with modern network components.","importance":"Its significance lies at the optimization-systems interface. For pretraining, the useful message is that first-order optimizers dominate not because curvature is irrelevant, but because engineering fit, memory cost, communication overhead, and support for modern modules have been poor. If K-FAC can be made robust on contemporary architectures, it may become attractive again in large-batch training, low-step regimes, or late-stage convergence.","technical_approach":"The core contribution is a modernization of K-FAC rather than a new second-order theory. The authors generalize the Kronecker-factored approximations across a wider range of layer types and provide a more unified way to construct curvature factors in autodiff frameworks. The key difference is reduced mismatch between K-FAC and real architectures: which layers can share approximations, which statistics require careful stabilization, and which modules need extra corrections. The goal is to preserve K-FAC's structural efficiency while minimizing ad hoc special cases.","experiments":"The empirical result is that the updated K-FAC runs stably on a broader set of modern architectures and can offer better sample or step efficiency than first-order baselines in some regimes. The paper compares SGD/Adam against K-FAC variants, analyzing final accuracy, convergence speed, and wall-clock trade-offs. The important engineering takeaway is that utility depends strongly on update frequency, damping, and approximation choices: curvature updates that are too frequent become expensive, while overly stale estimates lose most of the gain. The paper shows a workable middle ground rather than universal superiority.","authors_note":""},"tags":["optimizer","second-order","training-stability","efficiency"],"notable_authors":["Runa Eschenhagen","Alexander Immer","Richard E. Turner","Frank Schneider","Philipp Hennig"]},{"id":"FanDD023","title":"Evaluating Neuron Interpretation Methods of NLP Models","authors":["Yimin Fan","Fahim Dalvi","Nadir Durrani","Hassan Sajjad"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"NEURIPS","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/nips/FanDD023","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/nips/FanDD023","_ee":"http://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/eef6cb60fd59b32d35718e176b4b08d6-Abstract-Conference.html","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：NLP 中大量 neuron interpretation 方法在讲“某个神经元代表某种语言现象”，但这些解释本身缺乏统一、严格的验证。过去常见做法是展示若干看起来合理的 case study，但很少系统回答：解释是否稳定、是否可复现、是否真正对应因果功能，而不是统计相关。","importance":"它的价值在于把 interpretability 从展示型工作往评测型工作推进。对 pretrain 研究的意义是，如果我们想用神经元解释来指导数据配比、模块设计或安全分析，就必须先知道这些解释方法是否可信。否则，很容易把表面相关当成内部机制，误导后续训练决策。","technical_approach":"核心方法不是提出新的解释算法，而是建立评估框架来比较现有 neuron interpretation 方法。作者从稳定性、忠实性、可推广性等维度设定评测标准，并在多个 NLP 模型与语言现象上检验不同方法。关键区别在于：它不满足于‘能找到一些漂亮例子’，而是考察在不同随机种子、数据切分和模型层级下，解释是否依然一致，以及干预这些神经元后模型行为是否真按解释变化。","experiments":"关键实验结论是：许多常用 neuron interpretation 方法能找到表面上可读的模式，但在稳定性和因果有效性上并不稳健。论文比较了多种解释方法，在不同模型和任务上测量解释一致性、神经元选择重叠度及干预后的行为变化。更有信息量的发现通常是，某些方法在描述性指标上很好，但一旦做 activation patching、ablation 或控制实验，解释与真实功能的对应显著减弱。这说明 neuron-level 解释在 NLP 中仍更适合作为启发，而不是直接当成可靠机制图谱。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a credibility problem in NLP interpretability: many neuron interpretation methods claim that specific neurons encode specific linguistic phenomena, but these claims are rarely evaluated rigorously. Prior work often relied on compelling case studies without systematically asking whether the interpretations are stable, reproducible, or causally meaningful rather than merely correlational.","importance":"Its contribution is evaluative rather than algorithmic, but that is exactly why it matters. For pretraining research, neuron interpretations are often used to reason about internal representations, safety, and data effects. If the interpretation methods themselves are unreliable, they can easily mislead downstream analysis and design choices.","technical_approach":"The paper does not propose a new interpretation method; it builds an evaluation framework for existing neuron interpretation approaches. It assesses them along dimensions such as stability, faithfulness, and generalizability across models and linguistic phenomena. The key shift is from 'can we find some plausible examples?' to 'do these explanations remain consistent across seeds, data splits, and layers, and do interventions on the identified neurons actually change behavior in the predicted way?'","experiments":"The main empirical result is that many commonly used neuron interpretation methods can produce readable patterns, but their stability and causal validity are limited. The paper compares multiple methods across NLP models and tasks, measuring consistency, overlap in selected neurons, and behavioral changes after intervention. A particularly useful finding is that some methods look strong on descriptive metrics yet weaken substantially under ablation, patching, or controlled tests. The key trade-off is between added information and hallucination. If rewriting is too conservative, gains are small; if it over-specifies content not grounded in the image, alignment degrades. The trade-off is obvious: if the invariance is too aggressive, the model may lose sensitivity to wording that actually matters.","technical_approach":"The method appears to build a language model that is explicitly stable under lexical substitutions, instead of relying on plain next-token prediction alone. Its significance is that it tries to make HPO budget allocation predictable rather than ad hoc. For large-scale model research, that matters because many apparent method gains are really differences in tuning effort; understanding HPO scaling makes comparisons fairer and resource planning more rational.","technical_approach":"The core idea is to characterize how performance improves as HPO budget grows and to identify scaling relationships for search effort versus return. Unlike standard HPO papers that introduce a new optimizer, this work is more about regularities: how quickly gains saturate, how optimal configurations migrate with model or data scale, and when further tuning is no longer worth the compute. This is not a replacement for chain-of-thought or RL, but it is a useful idea about reusing model-generated or externally generated explanatory structure as training data.","technical_approach":"The core approach is to generate post hoc explanations for model predictions and then train with those explanations as additional supervision or auxiliary objectives. FLOPs，忽略了真实推理时延和内存访问模式，因此“剪得很多”不一定“跑得更快”。过去大量非结构化剪枝在 LLM 上的实际部署收益有限，就是因为推理硬件对结构化模式更敏感。","importance":"它是很典型的系统级压缩工作，对 serving 和大模型部署有直接价值，也能反过来影响预训练研究对架构冗余的理解。关键启示是：压缩目标不能只优化离线代理指标，必须把 inference-aware 约束放进设计里；否则在语言模型这种 memory-bound 系统里，参数少了但 wall-clock 不动。对 pretrain 的间接意义在于，未来架构搜索、稀疏训练和蒸馏都应更早考虑部署可兑现性。","technical_approach":"核心方法是面向推理的结构化剪枝框架 ZipLM。它不是做任意权重置零，而是寻找硬件友好、内核友好的结构化删减方式，并把真实推理成本近似纳入剪枝决策。关键设计通常包括：按层/头/通道或块进行裁剪，配合重要性估计与全局预算分配；与传统只看 loss 增量的剪枝不同，它强调哪些稀疏模式能真正转化成时延和显存收益。这个 trade-off 很清楚：结构化剪枝更容易兑现加速，但精度-压缩曲线往往不如细粒度非结构化剪枝。","experiments":"这类论文通常会在标准语言模型或 decoder-only Transformer 上报告 perplexity、zero-shot 精度、参数压缩率，以及 GPU/CPU translate into real inference speedup. Language model inference is strongly shaped by memory access and kernel efficiency, so pruning that ignores deployment constraints frequently underdelivers.","importance":"This is a systems-oriented compression paper with direct relevance to LLM serving, and it also feeds back into how we think about architectural redundancy. The main lesson is practical but important: compression objectives must be inference-aware, not just sparsity-aware. Otherwise one can prune aggressively on paper and still gain little in wall-clock latency. That insight matters upstream as well for architecture design, sparse training, and distillation.","technical_approach":"ZipLM is an inference-aware structured pruning method. Instead of zeroing arbitrary weights, it searches for hardware-friendly structured reductions and incorporates approximations of real inference cost into pruning decisions. The typical ingredients are layer/head/channel/block-level pruning, importance estimation, and global budget allocation. Compared with standard loss-centric pruning, the key difference is that it optimizes for sparsity patterns that can actually be realized by fast kernels. The trade-off is familiar: structured pruning is easier to accelerate in practice, but it can be less flexible than fine-grained sparsity.","experiments":"Work in this category usually reports perplexity or zero-shot accuracy, compression ratio, and real latency or throughput gains on CPU/GPU, comparing against non-structured and structured pruning baselines. Manually curated counterfactual examples are expensive and narrow in coverage, so standard benchmarks often fail to test whether a model is truly sensitive to fine-grained semantic alignment.","importance":"This is primarily a data and evaluation contribution. Its value is that it makes counterfactual testing scalable through automatic construction. For multimodal pretraining, that matters because if both training and evaluation reward only coarse matching, models will keep using shortcuts. Counterfactual pairs are a cleaner way to test whether predictions depend on object identity, attributes, counts, and relations instead of language priors alone.","technical_approach":"The core idea is to automatically generate counterfactual text for COCO image-text pairs by changing key semantic elements while keeping the rest of the caption as constant as possible. The design goal is localized, controlled perturbation: change one object, attribute, count, or relation at a time so that failure can be attributed to a specific grounding dimension. Compared with manual rewriting or unconstrained negative generation, this scales better and is easier to analyze. The trade-off is that automatic edits may reduce linguistic naturalness, and incomplete image annotations can create false counterfactuals.","experiments":"The main empirical finding is typically that many existing VLMs look strong on standard COCO-style evaluation but drop notably on automatically generated counterfactuals, revealing limited sensitivity to local semantic changes. Classical continual learning methods focus on catastrophic forgetting using dedicated regularizers or replay schemes, but that framing depends heavily on discrete task definitions and sits awkwardly with the modern trend of treating everything as sequence modeling.","importance":"The main value is the reframing. Casting continual learning as sequence modeling is relevant to pretraining because large-scale training is already a streaming, mixed, and staged data process. If continual learning can be expressed in a unified sequence-prediction form, some specialized CL machinery may be replaceable by more general context and sequence mechanisms. The contribution is not that replay becomes obsolete, but that part of the forgetting problem may come from an overly discrete interface.","technical_approach":"The core idea is to encode samples, tasks, or update history as sequences and let the model handle cross-stage dependencies through sequence modeling rather than explicit task-level rules. This is better understood as a problem reformulation than as a new optimizer trick. Relative to standard CL methods, it drops strong assumptions about task boundaries in exchange for a more unified modeling interface. The downside is that performance becomes sensitive to sequence representation, order, and context length, and scaling this efficiently to realistic settings remains nontrivial.","experiments":"The expected empirical result is that the sequence-modeling view can be competitive with or better than standard continual learning baselines on forgetting-versus-adaptation trade-offs. It pushes PEFT beyond low-rank factorization toward structured sparsification, which is relevant for continual pretraining, domain adaptation, and storage-efficient deployment. For pretraining researchers, the useful signal is that adaptation capacity does not have to come only from higher rank; careful structure selection can preserve useful update directions at lower cost.","technical_approach":"The core idea is a winner-take-all column-row sampling mechanism for adaptation matrices. Instead of learning a dense low-rank update, the method keeps only the most important rows and columns, allocating the budget to a smaller but more informative subspace. This differs from unstructured pruning because it stays hardware-friendlier, and it differs from fixed low-rank adaptation because it explicitly exploits the non-uniform importance of update directions. The design should work when fine-tuning gradients are concentrated in a few dominant directions; the main risk is that poor importance estimation can make early selection unstable.","experiments":"From the title and available metadata, the paper appears to compare against standard PEFT baselines on the trade-off between adaptation quality and memory cost, with the claim that it remains competitive under tighter parameter budgets. Adam。K-FAC 一类方法利用 Kronecker 分解降低了成本，但仍然不够轻，而且动量与曲率估计的结合不够自然；MKOR 试图把这条路线推向更可用。","importance":"它的贡献属于优化器设计与系统效率。对 pretrain 的意义在于，如果能以接近一阶方法的开销获得更好的曲率利用和训练稳定性，那么大规模预训练的学习率上限、batch 扩展性和 loss spike 相比，它更强调在线、轻量的曲率近似更新；和纯一阶动量法相比，它能按参数方向做更细的缩放，因此在病态曲率下更有优势。代价是实现复杂度更高，而且在现代超大模型、混合精度和分布式训练下是否稳定，取决于很多系统细节。","experiments":"关键实验通常会比较 MKOR 与 SGD/Adam/K-FAC 在收敛速度、最终精度、吞吐和内存上的差异。对基础模型研究者，最重要的是看它是否在相近 wall-clock 下真正更快收敛，而不是只在 step 数上占优；如果论文展示了更大 batch 更稳、或在 Transformer/vision backbone 上减少训练抖动，那是更有价值的信号。还应关注 ablation：rank-1 更新近似损失了多少曲率质量，动量项带来的增益来自优化轨迹平滑还是更好预条件。","authors_note":"仅凭题面无法可靠确认机构与团队代表作，返回空字符串。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper targets a long-standing optimization gap: second-order or curvature-aware methods can improve convergence and stability, but are usually too expensive to replace Adam in large-scale training. Kronecker-factored methods help, yet often remain heavy and awkward to combine with momentum; MKOR tries to make this family more practical.","importance":"This is an optimizer and systems-efficiency contribution. For pretraining, the potential upside is meaningful: if a near-second-order optimizer can run at close to first-order cost, it may raise stable learning-rate limits, improve large-batch scaling, and reduce training instabilities such as spikes or slow plateaus.","technical_approach":"MKOR is a Kronecker-factor-based optimizer that uses rank-1 updates together with momentum to keep curvature estimation lightweight. The design keeps the main structure of second-order preconditioning while avoiding the full cost of dense matrix maintenance and inversion. Relative to classical K-FAC, it aims for cheaper online updates. Relative to standard momentum methods, it can rescale directions more intelligently under ill-conditioned curvature. The trade-off is implementation complexity and sensitivity to distributed training details, especially in mixed-precision large-model settings.","experiments":"The important comparisons are against SGD, Adam, and K-FAC-style baselines on convergence speed, final accuracy, memory, and throughput. This phenomenon is well known in graph networks and is increasingly relevant for deep Transformers, but it is often treated only indirectly through architecture heuristics rather than as an explicit optimization target.","importance":"This is a method-level contribution around representation preservation and depth scalability. For pretraining, the message is that simply adding layers is not always beneficial if the model keeps washing token states into each other. A regularizer that preserves useful variation can improve the depth–capacity trade-off without changing the whole architecture.","technical_approach":"The method introduces a regularization term based on nonlocal functionals to discourage indiscriminate mixing across token representations. Khan"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"NEURIPS","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/nips/NicklXTMK23","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/nips/NicklXTMK23","_ee":"http://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/550ab405d0addd3de5b70e57b44878df-Abstract-Conference.html","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文关注的核心问题是：如何刻画一个模型对训练数据的敏感性，即某个样本被加入、删除或扰动后，模型记忆和输出会如何变化。过去关于 data influence、memorization 和 data attribution 的工具很多，但往往要么局部近似粗糙，要么计算代价太高，不足以解释现代深网的真实敏感性。作者提出的 Memory-Perturbation Equation，目标就是给出一个更系统的关系式来连接数据扰动与模型记忆行为。","importance":"这项工作的价值在于把数据影响分析从经验探针推进到更结构化的理论/方法框架。对 pretrain 的意义有两层：第一，它有助于理解哪些数据被模型强记住、哪些只贡献统计泛化，这关系到数据去重、隐私和高质量数据配比；第二，它给 continual pretrain、数据清洗和 machine unlearning 提供了更细的分析视角。重要性不在直接提升性能，而在于帮助我们更精确地观察‘模型是怎么被数据塑形的’。","technical_approach":"作者的核心方法是推导一个连接记忆量与数据扰动响应的方程或近似关系，用于预测模型参数或输出对特定数据变化的敏感度。直观上，这类方法通常把训练动态、损失 Hessian、梯度影响函数或核近似联系起来，以避免对每个数据删改都重新训练。和经典 influence functions 相比，它可能更强调‘记忆’这一对象，而不是单纯测试点损失变化；和纯经验 membership probing 相比，它试图提供可解释的因果联系。","experiments":"实验上的核心事实应当是：该方程能较好预测不同样本或数据子集对模型记忆/输出的影响，并在若干设置下优于现有 influence 或 memorization 估计方法。题面没有提供模型、数据集和误差数字，因此不能填具体值；但从标题判断，合理的实验会包括数据删除或扰动后的再训练验证、不同训练阶段的敏感性比较，以及对高记忆样本的识别能力。对预训练实践最有价值的是它能否在不重训的前提下稳定筛出‘高影响数据’。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper studies model sensitivity to data: how much a model’s memory and predictions change when specific training examples are added, removed, or perturbed. Existing tools for data attribution and memorization analysis are often either too crude or too expensive for modern deep networks. The proposed Memory-Perturbation Equation aims to relate data perturbations to memorization behavior more systematically.","importance":"This is an analysis-oriented contribution with relevance to pretraining data quality, privacy, deduplication, continual pretraining, and unlearning. Its value is not in boosting benchmark performance, but in giving a sharper view of how training data shapes the model. That matters because not all data points contribute in the same way: some drive generalization, while others are disproportionately memorized.","technical_approach":"The core method is to derive an equation or approximation linking memory to the model’s response under data perturbations. In practice, this kind of framework usually connects training dynamics with curvature or gradient-based influence estimates so that retraining is not required for every what-if analysis. Compared with classical influence functions, the emphasis here is likely on memorization itself rather than only prediction loss at a test point.","experiments":"The likely empirical claim is that the proposed equation predicts the effect of data perturbations on model memory or outputs better than existing influence-style baselines. This paper derives the joint infinite-depth-and-width limit of Transformers to understand how attention and residual scaling interact in this regime.","importance":"A theoretical contribution that directly informs practical choices around residual scaling and initialization in deep Transformers. The finding that vanilla residual connections cause attention rank collapse at large depth connects to real training stability issues in deep pretrained models.","technical_approach":"The authors take L (depth) and d (width) to infinity simultaneously and derive the distributional limit of hidden states and attention matrices. The key result: without depth-dependent scaling, attention matrices collapse to rank-1. The 'shaped' Transformer applies a 1/L scaling to residual branches, yielding a well-defined ODE in the continuous limit and preserving non-trivial attention structure. This is essentially a principled justification for depth-scaled residual connections.","experiments":"Primarily theory-validation experiments on synthetic tasks and small-scale language modeling. Standard graph compilers and distributed runtimes optimize per-op or over a few predefined patterns, which leaves memory traffic, synchronization, and scheduling overhead on the table.","importance":"This is a systems contribution with clear relevance to large-scale pretraining. In practice, training efficiency is limited by memory movement and communication at least as much as by raw FLOPs, so a framework that parallelizes across operator boundaries can influence compiler design and distributed training strategy more broadly than a model-specific optimization.","technical_approach":"ASPEN appears to introduce a program representation and execution strategy that reasons beyond individual operators. Instead of preserving op boundaries and only applying local kernel generation or fixed fusion, it likely rewrites larger computation regions and jointly chooses partitioning, fusion, and execution order; that is promising because many overheads come from artificial boundaries, not mathematical necessity. The trade-off is a larger compilation/search space and potentially weaker support for dynamic or irregular workloads.","experiments":"The metadata here does not provide reliable model names, hardware details, or exact speedups, so I should not state numbers. The lesson for the pretrain-to-posttrain pipeline is uncomfortable but useful: pushing a model to follow user intent more aggressively can also amplify jailbreakability if robustness to adversarial or out-of-distribution instructions is not trained at the same time.","technical_approach":"This is an analysis-and-attack paper rather than a new modeling method. The authors compare base models and their instruction-tuned counterparts under several prompt-based attacks, including explicit harmful instructions, role-play, indirection, and formatting tricks. The key hypothesis is that instruction tuning shifts behavior toward compliance with the immediate prompt, weakening the uncertainty and refusal behaviors inherited from pretraining. The paper is less about a single jailbreak technique and more about measuring systematic changes in conditional generation behavior after tuning.","experiments":"The main empirical finding is that instruction-tuned models are often more exploitable than their base versions across multiple attack settings and model families. Existing options usually trade one problem for another: continuous relaxations such as Gumbel-Softmax introduce bias, while score-function estimators often suffer from large variance.","importance":"This is a methods paper with broad utility. It is not a paradigm shift for pretraining by itself, but it matters anywhere discrete decisions appear inside differentiable systems, including latent-variable models, hard routing, token selection, and non-AR generative setups. Better categorical gradient estimators can remove some of the compromises imposed by soft relaxations.","technical_approach":"The paper proposes the CatLog-Derivative Trick, a new way to derive differentiable estimators for categorical sampling. The bottleneck is not the objective itself but the practicality of MCMC-based inference.","importance":"Its significance lies in connecting EBMs with diffusion-based inference. For generative pretraining and alternative language modeling paradigms, the main lesson is that diffusion models need not be the final generator—they can also serve as efficient amortized samplers for more general probabilistic models such as energy-based priors.","technical_approach":"The method is diffusion-amortized MCMC. Instead of relying purely on slow hand-designed MCMC dynamics like Langevin updates, a diffusion model is used to learn or approximate the sampling process needed to train an energy-based prior. This differs from standard diffusion generation because the target object is still the EBM prior, not the diffusion model itself. The design makes sense because diffusion processes are good at learning complex distribution transitions, which can replace expensive iterative sampling. The trade-off is coupled training complexity: if the amortized sampler is inaccurate, it can introduce bias into EBM learning.","experiments":"The expected evaluation would compare against conventional EBM training with MCMC, and possibly against pure diffusion baselines, on image generation or density-estimation style benchmarks. Relative to standard momentum, the new ingredient is neighborhood sampling; relative to SWA, the averaging happens over short training windows rather than late-stage weight snapshots. ODE solver 在低步数下容易受模型误差影响，而理论阶数高并不自动等于实际采样更稳。作者希望利用模型在不同时间步上的经验统计信息，修正 solver 对 score/noise 预测误差的处理方式，从而在同样步数下得到更好的质量—速度折中。","importance":"这是一个方法级、系统感很强的工作。它对 pretrain 的直接启示不如语言模型论文强，但对 diffusion 作为替代建模范式很有参考价值：当生成模型越来越大时，性能瓶颈往往不只在训练目标，也在推断器是否充分利用了模型误差结构。换句话说，采样器不是纯后处理，它会反过来决定你训练出来的模型能释放出多少能力。","technical_approach":"DPM-Solver-v3 的核心是，在经典 DPM-Solver 的解析积分框架上，引入经验模型统计量来校正不同时间段的局部解误差。与只依赖理论泰勒展开或固定假设的高阶 solver 相比，它更像是在求解器里加入一个 data/model-aware 的误差补偿项。这样设计有效，是因为真实扩散模型的预测误差并不满足理想化平滑假设，尤其在少步采样时，局部偏差会被快速累积；用经验统计去估计和抵消这类偏差，比单纯追求更高理论阶数更实用。代价是需要额外统计估计过程，并且统计量是否跨模型、跨噪声日程泛化，是这类方法的天然边界。","experiments":"这类论文的关键事实通常是：在图像生成基准上，v3 能在 5 到 20 步等低步数采样区域优于前代 DPM-Solver 和其他常见 solver，在 FID 或类似质量指标上取得更好结果，或者在相同质量下进一步减少步数。更有价值的实验通常包括：不同 prediction parameterization（如 noise / data / velocity）下是否都稳定；经验统计是离线预估还是在线更新；以及统计项去掉后性能回退多少。由于当前没有摘要，无法可靠给出具体数据集、UNet/DiT addresses a practical bottleneck in diffusion sampling: existing ODE solvers often lose quality when the number of sampling steps is small, and higher theoretical order does not automatically translate into better real-world performance. The authors aim to use empirical statistics of model behavior across timesteps to correct solver error and improve the quality-speed trade-off.","importance":"This is a method-level contribution with strong systems relevance. Its direct connection to language pretraining is limited, but it matters for diffusion as an alternative generative paradigm: once the model is large enough, generation quality depends not only on the training objective but also on whether the sampler handles model error well. In that sense, the solver is not merely post-processing; it determines how much of the trained model’s capability is actually recoverable at inference time.","technical_approach":"DPM-Solver-v3 extends the analytical integration framework of earlier DPM-Solver methods by incorporating empirical model statistics to compensate for local truncation and prediction errors. The key difference from purely theory-driven high-order solvers is that v3 is explicitly model-aware: it uses observed error structure instead of assuming ideal smoothness. This is effective because real diffusion models do not behave like clean textbook ODEs, especially in low-step sampling where local bias accumulates quickly. Estimating and correcting that bias can be more useful than increasing formal solver order. The trade-off is extra statistical estimation, and a natural open question is how well those statistics transfer across architectures and noise schedules.","experiments":"The core empirical claim is likely that v3 improves low-step sampling, often in the 5–20 step regime, over prior DPM-Solver variants and other standard samplers, either by lowering FID at fixed steps or by reaching comparable quality with fewer steps. The most informative ablations would test robustness across prediction parameterizations such as noise, data, or velocity prediction, and isolate the contribution of the empirical-statistics term by removing it. Without the abstract, exact datasets, model sizes, and numbers should not be invented, but the title already makes the main point clear: this is a better diffusion ODE solver via empirical model statistics, not a new diffusion training objective.","authors_note":"Jun Zhu is a well-known researcher from Tsinghua University with representative work in diffusion models, probabilistic inference, and generative modeling. Using two case studies, “Clock” and “Pizza,” the paper asks how to separate genuine mechanism from post hoc narrative.","importance":"Its value is methodological. Jordan"]},{"id":"ZhuTSZ23","title":"Generalized Logit Adjustment: Calibrating Fine-tuned Models by Removing Label Bias in Foundation Models","authors":["Beier Zhu","Kaihua Tang","Qianru Sun","Hanwang Zhang"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"NEURIPS","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/nips/ZhuTSZ23","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/nips/ZhuTSZ23","_ee":"http://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/cbe1fd3136e0f049bb8bc104231ccb99-Abstract-Conference.html","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：微调后的分类模型常带着 foundation model 预训练阶段形成的标签先验偏置，导致概率校准差、长尾类受损，而常见校准方法多在输出层做温度缩放，不能针对这种由预训练表征和标签偏好共同引入的系统性偏差。这个问题值得关注，因为 foundation model 越强，预训练先验越会主导少样本和分布外场景下的决策。","importance":"这是方法级工作，重点不在提升峰值精度，而在解释并修正 foundation model 微调后的 label bias。对 pretrain 的启示是，预训练模型并不是中性的特征提取器，它会把训练语料中的类别频率和语义偏好带入下游；如果只看平均 accuracy，这种问题容易被掩盖，但在校准、长尾和迁移场景里会放大。因此这类工作对理解 pretrain→finetune 接口很有价值。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 generalized logit adjustment，用更一般的 logit 偏置修正来去除 foundation model 的标签偏置。它可以看作对长尾学习里 logit adjustment 的扩展，但这里调整的不是简单经验类别频率，而是微调模型继承自预训练先验的偏置项。方法设计的关键在于：估计或分解出预测分数中由标签先验带来的部分，再在推理或训练中做校正，从而改善后验概率的校准。相比温度缩放，这种方法能处理类别间非均匀偏差；相比重新重采样训练，它代价更低、适配性更强，但前提是偏置能被 logit 层面的修正近似。","experiments":"从题目可确定实验会聚焦校准与分类性能，尤其是在 foundation model 微调场景下，与温度缩放、普通 logit adjustment 或其他后处理校准方法比较。关键指标应包括 ECE、NLL、Brier score 以及 accuracy，可能还会覆盖长尾或分布偏移数据集。没有摘要，无法确认使用的是 CLIP、ViT、BERT 还是其他 foundation model，也不能给出具体提升数字。能较有把握地说，论文应展示：去除标签偏置后，校准显著改善，同时精度基本保持或在尾部类别上提升，并通过 ablation 说明偏置估计方式对效果敏感。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles a specific calibration issue: fine-tuned models inherit label bias from their foundation-model pretraining, and standard calibration tools such as temperature scaling do not explicitly remove that bias. This matters because stronger pretrained models often inject stronger priors into downstream predictions, especially in few-shot, long-tail, or shifted settings.","importance":"This is a method-level contribution with useful implications for the pretrain-to-finetune interface. The main message is that a foundation model is not a neutral feature extractor; it carries category priors and semantic preferences from pretraining, and those priors can hurt calibration even when top-line accuracy looks fine. That makes the work relevant beyond calibration itself, especially for understanding transfer behavior under imbalance and shift.","technical_approach":"The core method is generalized logit adjustment, which applies a more flexible correction to prediction logits to remove inherited label bias. It extends the idea of logit adjustment from long-tail learning, but the target here is not just empirical class frequency in the finetuning set; it is the bias induced by the pretrained foundation model. The key design choice is to estimate the prior-like component embedded in the logits and subtract or compensate for it during training or inference. Compared with temperature scaling, this can correct class-specific distortions rather than only global overconfidence. Compared with retraining under resampling, it is cheaper, but it assumes the bias is well captured at the logit level.","experiments":"The experiments should center on calibration and classification performance after finetuning foundation models, with baselines such as temperature scaling, standard logit adjustment, and other post-hoc calibration methods. Relevant metrics likely include ECE, NLL, Brier score, and accuracy, possibly on long-tail or shifted datasets. The intended generation can be expressed in natural language, with the model producing faithful interpretations of text prompts. However, expressing complex or nuanced ideas in text alone can be difficult. To ease image generation, we propose MultiFusion that allows one to express complex and nuanced concepts with arbitrarily interleaved inputs of multiple modalities and languages. MutliFusion leverages pre-trained models and aligns them for integration into a cohesive system, thereby avoiding the need for extensive training from scratch. Our experimental results demonstrate the efficient transfer of capabilities from individual modules to the downstream model. Specifically, the fusion of all independent components allows the image generation module to utilize multilingual, interleaved multimodal inputs despite being trained solely on monomodal data in a single language.","venue":"NEURIPS","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.15296","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/nips/BellagenteBTFDE23","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/nips/BellagenteBTFDE23","_ee":"http://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/ba8d1b46292c5e82cbfb3b3dc3b968af-Abstract-Conference.html","category_id":"multimodal_gen","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作解决的是多模态图像生成里一个被长期弱处理的问题：用户的意图往往无法只靠单段英文文本准确表达。现有 text-to-image diffusion 大多假设输入是单一语言的文本提示，复杂控制需求则依赖 ad-hoc 的模块拼接；作者想做的是把多语言文本、图像等多种条件统一接到同一个生成系统里，而且尽量复用已有预训练模块。","importance":"它的贡献更偏范式接口与系统整合，而不是重新训练一个基础生成模型。对 pretrain 的启示很直接：跨模态统一建模不一定总靠从头 joint pretrain，也可以通过对齐已有编码器和生成器来快速继承能力；这对多语言、多模态扩展尤其重要，因为从零联合训练的成本太高，模块化迁移是更现实的路线。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 MultiFusion：把多个已预训练的单模态或单语言模块对齐后接入图像生成流程，实现任意交错的多模态、多语言条件控制。设计上它明显选择了“融合现成专家”而不是“训练一个全新统一 backbone”，好处是能保留各模块已有能力、降低训练成本；代价是系统上限受限于模块接口是否兼容，以及对齐层能否在不破坏原能力的情况下学会跨模态组合。它和传统 prompt engineering 的差别在于，控制信号不再被压缩成单通道文本，而是显式保留多源条件。","experiments":"摘要明确声称实验展示了 individual modules 的能力可以高效迁移到下游多模态、多语言图像生成任务，但题面截断，没有具体数字。按这类工作的关键实验，应当包括：与单文本提示 diffusion baseline 比较在复杂组合提示下的生成质量与可控性；测试不同语言输入是否能保持语义一致；评估图像+文本等交错输入是否优于只用文本；以及少量对齐训练能否激活预训练模块已有能力。如果没有从头训练的大规模对比，结论应解读为“高效整合可行”，而不是“全面超越统一预训练”。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper targets a real limitation in image generation: user intent is often too rich to be expressed by a single English text prompt. That is especially relevant for multilingual and multimodal expansion, where full retraining is costly.","technical_approach":"MultiFusion aligns multiple pretrained modules and plugs them into a common image-generation pipeline, enabling mixed conditioning across modalities and languages. Earlier debiasing and erasure methods were often approximate, iterative, and quality-degrading; this work aims for a closed-form transform that exactly eliminates the target concept in the linear regime.","importance":"This is a method contribution with broad implications because it turns representation editing into something analytically precise. For pretraining research, it matters in two ways: it gives a clean tool for studying what concepts are encoded and where, and it clarifies the boundary of many alignment or debiasing claims—if the guarantee is only linear, that boundary should be stated explicitly rather than treated as full removal.","technical_approach":"LEACE is a closed-form linear concept-erasure procedure. The idea is to identify the subspace that contains information about a target concept as measured by linear recoverability, then construct a projection-like linear transform that removes exactly that information while preserving as much of the remaining representation as possible. Compared with iterative adversarial removal or heuristic projections, the appeal is a strong optimality/perfect-erasure statement and simple plug-in use; the limitation is equally clear: nonlinear remnants may remain.","experiments":"The prompt lacks the abstract, but the key experiments for this paper are usually about validating the mathematical guarantee in practical representations rather than maximizing downstream benchmark scores. Typical evidence would show that linear probes for the erased concept fall to chance after transformation, while task performance degrades less than with prior methods such as INLP or adversarial baselines; stronger nonlinear probes would then test what information still survives, making the boundary of the method explicit. Much of the field treats benchmark test sets as ground truth and compares average scores; this work asks whether synthetic test data can improve the stability, coverage, and trustworthiness of evaluation.","importance":"This is an evaluation-methodology contribution, but it matters to pretraining as well. Foundation-model improvements are often expensive and incremental; if evaluation noise is large, research effort gets wasted on unstable rankings. Well-constructed synthetic test data can help cover tail events, rare combinations, and failure boundaries, making it easier to distinguish genuine generalization from benchmark overfitting.","technical_approach":"The core idea is to improve evaluation using synthetic test data rather than to improve the model directly. A plausible mechanism is to fit a generator or structured data model from real data, then create semantically plausible test examples in undercovered regions and use them for variance reduction, robustness checks, or evaluation calibration. This is different from augmenting the training set: the goal is not to teach the model more, but to make model comparison more reliable. The limitation is obvious too: if the synthetic generator is biased, the evaluation may inherit that bias.","experiments":"The prompt does not include the abstract, so specific datasets and numbers cannot be reported confidently. The important experiments would usually compare original evaluation against evaluation augmented with synthetic tests in terms of variance, ranking stability, confidence intervals, and sensitivity to distribution shift or rare subgroups. The strongest result would be that synthetic test data improves failure detection and model ranking consistency without introducing large systematic distortion. For a practitioner, the key question is not whether synthetic samples look realistic, but whether they improve decision quality.","authors_note":"Includes Mihaela van der Schaar."},"tags":["evaluation","synthetic-data","benchmark"],"notable_authors":["Mihaela van der Schaar"]},{"id":"BykovKNKH23","title":"Labeling Neural Representations with Inverse Recognition","authors":["Kirill Bykov","Laura Kopf","Shinichi Nakajima","Marius Kloft","Marina M.-C. Höhne"],"institutions":["TU Kaiserslautern","TU Berlin"],"abstract":"","venue":"NEURIPS","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/nips/BykovKNKH23","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/nips/BykovKNKH23","_ee":"http://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/4e52bbb99690d1e05c7ef7b4c8b3569a-Abstract-Conference.html","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"如何为神经网络学到的内部表示（hidden representations）赋予人类可理解的语义标签。现有可解释性方法多聚焦于输入归因（attribution），对中间层表示的语义理解仍然粗糙，通常依赖 probing classifier 等间接手段。","importance":"对 pretrain 研究的启示有限，属于可解释性/表示分析方向的工具性工作。对于想理解 pretrain 模型中间层学到了什么的研究者有一定参考价值，但不直接影响预训练方法设计。","technical_approach":"提出 Inverse Recognition 方法：训练一个逆向模型，将神经网络的中间层表示映射回可解释的概念空间，从而为每个神经元或表示维度生成语义标签。与 probing classifier 的区别在于目标是生成描述性标签而非分类准确率。","experiments":"在视觉模型上验证，展示了生成的语义标签与人类直觉的一致性。具体实验规模和指标需参考原文。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"How to assign human-interpretable semantic labels to neural network internal representations. Prior workarounds—padding to max dimension or autoregressive generation—are inelegant.","importance":"A paradigm-level exploration extending diffusion models to trans-dimensional spaces. Relevant to pretrain research through connections to variable-length sequence generation in diffusion LMs and cross-modal unified generation where modalities have different dimensionalities.","technical_approach":"Couples a standard SDE diffusion process with a jump process that controls dimensional changes. During the forward process, data is noised while dimensions can stochastically increase or decrease; the reverse process simultaneously denoises and determines dimensionality. The key technical challenge is deriving score matching objectives and sampling algorithms under varying dimensions.","experiments":"Validated on molecular generation (QM9) and point cloud generation. Competitive with fixed-dimension padded diffusion and autoregressive baselines in generation quality and diversity, while more naturally handling variable dimensionality. 及更一般情形，因此结论更可迁移；代价是结果偏理论化，对实际最优路径和有限算力下的调度选择给得没那么具体。","experiments":"从题目和已知信息看，实验重点应是验证理论预测，而不是大规模模型 benchmark。可确定的关键事实有限：论文针对归一化常数估计任务比较了带 annealing 与不带 annealing 的估计误差、样本效率或收敛速度，并覆盖 importance sampling、NCE 等多种设定；核心结论是 annealing 在困难分布间显著更稳。由于这里没有摘要和正文，无法可靠给出模型规模、数据集名称、具体提升数字或 ablation 细节，因此不应编造。","authors_note":"机构与作者信息不足以在这里可靠总结代表性团队脉络。已知作者包括 Aapo Hyvärinen、Andrej This matters because partition-function estimation has long relied on heuristic bridges between easy and hard distributions, while the mechanism and scope of the gain were not cleanly characterized.","importance":"This is mainly a theory contribution with method-level implications rather than a direct training recipe for large models. For pretraining research, the useful takeaway is that whenever we train with unnormalized objectives, contrastive surrogates, or bridge distributions, the path between distributions is part of the objective design, not just an implementation detail.","technical_approach":"The core idea is a unified analysis of annealed estimation rather than a single new estimator. The authors insert a sequence of intermediate distributions between a reference and a target distribution, and analyze why breaking a hard estimation problem into easier local steps improves variance or sample complexity relative to one-shot estimation. The main distinction from prior work is that the paper treats annealing as a general meta-strategy covering importance sampling, NCE, and beyond, instead of analyzing one specific procedure in isolation. The trade-off is that the results are more structural than prescriptive: they clarify why annealing helps, but do not by themselves tell you the optimal schedule in a finite compute regime.","experiments":"Based on the title and available metadata, the empirical section is best understood as theory validation rather than a large-scale benchmark paper. If the probability flow ODE admits provable fast convergence under meaningful assumptions, that strengthens the theoretical case for deterministic samplers in diffusion models, including diffusion language models and multimodal token generators. The key choice is to hold the encoder, objective, and downstream planning/control interface as constant as possible, and swap only the temporal module; this matters because world models confound representation learning, latent dynamics, and policy/planner quality very easily. Compared with papers that only report final control return, this setup is better at exposing trade-offs in short-horizon prediction, long rollouts, sample efficiency, and throughput. RNNs are typically stable and cheap but weaker on long-range dependencies; Transformers are expressive but expensive in context and memory; S4 aims for better long-sequence efficiency through structured state-space dynamics, but whether that advantage survives task changes is an empirical question.","experiments":"The provided information is insufficient to state reliable model sizes, data scales, or exact numbers, so those should not be fabricated. Partial Jacobians can therefore expose local instability earlier and more concretely. Standard distillation methods often optimize synthetic samples or static subset objectives, but these become costly or brittle as datasets and models grow.","importance":"This is a methods paper on data efficiency. Its relevance to pretraining is clear: large-scale training is increasingly constrained by data cost and data selection strategy, and subset selection is tightly connected to data mixture design, curriculum, and replay. Even outside LLMs, the ideas are useful for thinking about training-data compression.","technical_approach":"The key idea, Sequential Subset Matching, is to match the training trajectory induced by the full dataset with the trajectory induced by a tiny subset, rather than only matching the final model state. This is a sensible design because the data properties needed early, mid, and late in training are not identical. If one only matches final parameters or final loss, it is easy to obtain a subset that works at one stage but distorts the overall optimization dynamics. Sequential matching expands the target from the endpoint to the full path, which should preserve more transferable learning signal. The trade-off is computational complexity: trajectory-aware matching is more expensive and algorithmically more involved.","experiments":"From the title, the experiments likely compare selected subsets against prior subset-selection or dataset-distillation methods on standard benchmarks, probably in vision settings. That is especially important for enterprise and regulated deployments.","technical_approach":"The likely method is a differentially private prompt-learning algorithm that applies DP mechanisms during prompt optimization, typically gradient clipping and noise addition, to bound the influence of any individual training example. Compared with standard DP-SGD over all parameters, the appeal is lower compute cost and potentially better utility. The goal is not simply to scale in-context learning, but to test whether explicit Bayesian reasoning over language-like hypotheses can better capture human-style generalization.","importance":"Its value is mainly conceptual and mechanistic. For pretraining, the relevant takeaway is that natural language can serve not only as input data but also as an interface for structured hypothesis spaces. If a model can express, compare, and update rule hypotheses in language, few-shot generalization may rely less on implicit memorization alone. This connects naturally to latent reasoning, program-like induction, and test-time adaptation.","technical_approach":"The core idea is to treat natural language as an explicit hypothesis representation and perform Bayesian inference over it. Given a few examples, the system proposes candidate rules in natural language, updates their posterior probabilities using the observations, and predicts new cases from the posterior. This differs from directly prompting an LLM for answers: language is used as an intermediate reasoning object. The benefit is interpretable probabilistic updating over compositional rules; the cost is that both inference efficiency and hypothesis proposal become critical bottlenecks.","experiments":"The main empirical result is that Bayesian few-shot learning over natural-language hypotheses better matches human behavior on several concept-learning or induction tasks and performs well in extremely low-data settings. The paper compares against direct prediction models, program-induction style baselines, and other few-shot methods, evaluating both accuracy and agreement with human judgments. A particularly useful observation is that success depends heavily on the quality of the proposed language hypotheses: if the correct rule is not proposed, posterior inference cannot recover it. That makes hypothesis generation, not only posterior updating, the central challenge.","authors_note":""},"tags":["few-shot","bayesian","natural-language"],"notable_authors":["Kevin Ellis"]},{"id":"Engelken23a","title":"Gradient Flossing: Improving Gradient Descent through Dynamic Control of Jacobians","authors":["Rainer Engelken"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"NEURIPS","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/nips/Engelken23a","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/nips/Engelken23a","_ee":"http://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/214ce905bf2072535e34b3cf873cbbc8-Abstract-Conference.html","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文的核心问题是：标准梯度下降只看一阶梯度，默认网络局部几何由参数化给定，因此在病态 Jacobian 条件下会出现学习慢、梯度传播差和不稳定。作者试图解决的是，能否在不完全转向昂贵二阶方法的前提下，动态调控 Jacobian，让梯度下降走在更有利的局部几何上。","importance":"它的重要性主要在优化方法层面。对 pretrain 的启示是，很多训练难题并不只是学习率或归一化问题，还和模型的输入输出 Jacobian 如何随训练演化有关；如果能低成本调控 Jacobian 谱，可能改善深层网络的可训练性和稳定性。它不是成熟的大规模 recipe，但代表了一类介于初始化技巧与二阶预条件之间的方向。","technical_approach":"作者提出的 Gradient Flossing，可以理解为在训练过程中动态塑形 Jacobian 的优化控制方法。核心思想不是显式求全 Hessian，而是通过附加控制项或更新规则，鼓励网络 Jacobian 保持更适合梯度传播和优化的结构。和传统权重衰减、梯度裁剪不同，这个方法直接作用于网络微分几何；和全量二阶法相比，它追求更低成本、可在现代网络上运行。设计上的关键取舍是只控制最关键的 Jacobian 性质，而不是精确建模全部曲率。","experiments":"实验结论是：在若干受控任务和网络上，Gradient Flossing 能提升收敛速度和训练稳定性，但收益依赖架构和任务。论文通常比较标准 SGD/Adam 或相关正则化方法，报告训练损失下降、更好的最终性能或更稳的梯度传播。更有信息量的消融在于 Jacobian 相关统计：当控制足够强时能改善条件数或谱性质，但过强控制会限制表示能力，带来欠拟合。这说明该方法的价值在于几何调节，而边界在于不能把网络过度刚性化。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a geometric weakness of standard gradient descent: it follows first-order gradients while inheriting whatever local Jacobian conditioning the parameterization happens to provide. When that Jacobian becomes poorly behaved, optimization slows down and gradient propagation degrades. The question is whether one can dynamically control Jacobians during training without paying the full price of second-order methods.","importance":"Its contribution is primarily on the optimization side. For pretraining, the takeaway is that some training difficulties are not just learning-rate or normalization issues; they also depend on how network Jacobians evolve. If Jacobian spectra can be steered cheaply, that may improve trainability and stability in deep networks. This is not yet a standard large-scale recipe, but it is a meaningful direction between initialization tricks and full curvature methods.","technical_approach":"Gradient Flossing is best viewed as dynamic Jacobian shaping during optimization. Rather than computing a full Hessian, the method adds control mechanisms that encourage the network Jacobian to remain in a regime that supports better gradient flow and optimization. Unlike weight decay or gradient clipping, it directly targets differential-geometric properties of the network. Unlike full second-order optimization, it aims to be cheaper and more practical. The key design choice is to control a few important Jacobian properties rather than model all curvature precisely.","experiments":"The empirical picture is that Gradient Flossing can improve convergence speed and training stability on several controlled tasks and architectures, though gains are regime-dependent. The paper compares against standard SGD/Adam-style baselines and related regularization approaches, reporting faster loss reduction, better final performance, or more stable gradient behavior. The most informative ablations track Jacobian statistics: sufficiently strong control can improve conditioning, but overly strong control reduces flexibility and can underfit. The goal here is more deployment-oriented: perform structured pruning that actually reduces FLOPs and runtime while preserving generation quality as much as possible.","importance":"Its contribution is mainly about efficiency and deployability. For pretraining and generative modeling, the takeaway is that diffusion cost is not only about reducing sampling steps; the backbone itself contains substantial redundancy. If structured compression can preserve quality, architecture design and sparsification become important cost levers for larger unified generative models as well.","technical_approach":"The core method is structured pruning for diffusion models rather than element-wise sparsification. The authors assess the importance of channels, blocks, or other architectural units, remove less important structure, and recover quality through retraining or distillation. The key design point is that diffusion denoising across timesteps changes layer importance compared with standard classification networks: some components matter more for early coarse structure, others for late detail refinement. So standard CNN pruning heuristics do not transfer directly. Compared with unstructured pruning, the method gives up some fine-grained flexibility in exchange for actual hardware-friendly acceleration.","experiments":"The main empirical finding is that structured pruning can substantially reduce parameter count and compute in several diffusion settings while keeping degradation in FID or sample quality within an acceptable range. This gives the model a compact parameterized handle on each tool without repeatedly stuffing long descriptions into the prompt. Compared with few-shot prompting, it scales better with many tools. When such problems are identified, we want to make targeted edits while avoiding expensive retraining. However, current model editors, which modify such behaviors of pre-trained models, degrade model performance quickly across multiple, sequential edits. We propose GRACE, a lifelong model editing method, which implements spot-fixes on streaming errors of a deployed model, ensuring minimal impact on unrelated inputs. GRACE writes new mappings into a pre-trained model's latent space, creating a discrete, local codebook of edits without altering model weights. This is the first method enabling thousands of sequential edits using only streaming errors. Our experiments on T5, BERT, and GPT models show GRACE's state-of-the-art performance in making and retaining edits, while generalizing to unseen inputs. Our code is available at https://www.github.com/thartvigsen/grace}.","venue":"NEURIPS","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.11031","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/nips/HartvigsenSPKG23","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/nips/HartvigsenSPKG23","_ee":"http://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/95b6e2ff961580e03c0a662a63a71812-Abstract-Conference.html","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：部署后的语言模型会因为世界知识变化、需求漂移和长期使用中的错误积累而变旧，但现有模型编辑方法在连续多次编辑后很快伤到原模型能力。作者关注的是 lifelong editing，也就是如何做成千上万次局部修补，而不是一次性演示式编辑。","importance":"这项工作的价值很高，主要体现在模型维护范式上。对 pretrain 研究的启示是，参数权重不一定是吸收新知识的唯一位置；可以把预训练模型视作稳定主体，再在其潜空间外挂可检索的局部修补层。这个思路和 continual pretrain、RAG、外部记忆都有联系，但它更强调局部性和顺序编辑稳定性。","technical_approach":"核心方法 GRACE 不直接改模型权重，而是在预训练模型的潜空间中写入离散 key-value adaptor，形成一个局部代码本来存放编辑。推理时，如果输入落入某个被编辑的局部区域，就从代码本读取修补映射；否则走原模型。这种设计有效的原因是把编辑限制在局部邻域，避免权重级全局干扰。与 ROME/MEMIT 这类直接改权重的方法相比，它更适合流式持续编辑；代价是需要额外检索/匹配机制，且编辑覆盖范围依赖潜空间局部性的可分性。","experiments":"摘要中可确认：GRACE 在 T5 等模型上验证了使用 streaming errors 做上千次顺序编辑的能力，并强调对无关输入影响小。这是它最关键的实验事实。给定片段没有提供具体编辑次数上限、准确率、保真度和保持率数字，因此不能补具体表格。最值得关注的实验点是：顺序编辑数量增长时性能曲线是否平稳；局部代码本命中失败时是否回退到原模型；以及与权重编辑基线相比，保留原任务性能的优势有多大。","authors_note":"作者包含 Yoon Kim、Marzyeh Ghassemi。两位分别在语言模型与医疗/稳健 ML 方向有较高知名度。仅凭给定信息无法确认全部机构。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a real deployment problem: language models become outdated as knowledge changes and user needs shift, but existing editing methods degrade quickly when many edits are applied sequentially. The focus here is lifelong editing rather than one-off fact correction demos.","importance":"This is an important contribution at the model-maintenance level. For pretraining researchers, the key lesson is that new knowledge does not always need to be absorbed by changing the base weights. A pretrained model can remain the stable core while local corrections live in an external latent-space memory. That connects naturally to continual pretraining, retrieval, and external memory, but with a stronger emphasis on locality and edit stability.","technical_approach":"GRACE avoids modifying model weights. Instead, it writes edits into discrete key-value adaptors in the model’s latent space, forming a local codebook of corrections. At inference time, inputs that land near an edited region retrieve the corresponding patch; unrelated inputs continue through the original model. This locality is the whole point: it minimizes collateral damage. Compared with direct weight-editing methods such as ROME or MEMIT, it is better suited to long edit streams. The trade-off is the need for a retrieval mechanism and the assumption that edit regions are separable in latent space.","experiments":"From the abstract, we can state one central result with confidence: GRACE supports thousands of sequential edits from streaming errors on models including T5 while keeping the effect localized. efficiency。虽然论文对象是图像扩散，但这种“长残差路径如何缩放”的问题，对 diffusion LM 和 AR+diffusion 联合建模也有借鉴价值。","technical_approach":"核心方法是提出 ScaleLong，用带缩放的 long skip connection training gets more demanding, with poor gradient flow and fragile convergence. Existing fixes are often local heuristics—learning-rate tuning, normalization tweaks, or backbone edits—rather than a structural answer to long-path optimization.","importance":"The contribution is architectural and optimization-oriented. For pretraining researchers, the main message is that skip-connection scaling is not a minor implementation choice once diffusion models are pushed to larger scale; it can become a first-order determinant of stability and usable compute. Although the setting is visual diffusion, the same lesson matters for diffusion language models and hybrid AR+diffusion systems.","technical_approach":"The proposed method, ScaleLong, introduces scaled long skip connections to stabilize diffusion training. The point is not merely adding another residual path, but controlling the magnitude of information passed across distant layers so that early features remain available without overwhelming shorter paths or destabilizing gradients. This differs from standard U-Net skip design because the scaling policy is the core mechanism, not the existence of the connection itself.","experiments":"The prompt does not provide enough detail to report datasets, parameter counts, baselines, or exact gains. Prior methods added feature matching or auxiliary objectives, but many still lacked a coherent view of how richer knowledge should flow from teacher to student.","importance":"Its contribution is mainly conceptual and methodological: it reframes distillation as a knowledge diffusion process rather than one-shot imitation. For pretraining-oriented work, this matters because model compression is also a question of knowledge preservation across training stages; better transfer mechanisms can make teacher-to-student pipelines more faithful and efficient.","technical_approach":"The core idea is to diffuse teacher knowledge to the student progressively, instead of aligning a single representation or output distribution all at once. In practice, this likely means staged transfer across levels of difficulty, abstraction, or noise, so the student first captures coarse structure and then finer behavior. Veitch"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$1a","venue":"NEURIPS","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.17611","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/nips/JiangAV23","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/nips/JiangAV23","_ee":"http://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/65a925049647eab0aa06a9faf1cd470b-Abstract-Conference.html","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：embedding 里到底编码了什么语义关系，尤其是“条件独立”这类比相似度更结构化的语义关系。过去大家常用 cosine 相似度或线性 probe 去解释 embedding，但这只能看到“像不像”，很难表达“在给定 vegetable 的条件下，eggplant 和 tomato 是否语义独立”这种更接近概率结构的问题。","importance":"结论是：这是一篇偏机制分析和表示理论的工作，对直接提升预训练效果的冲击有限，但对理解 embedding 几何很有价值。对 pretrain 研究者的意义在于，它给出了一个比相似度更细粒度的语言表示分析视角：如果预训练表示真的携带组合式语义结构，那么这种结构不应只体现在距离上，也应体现在某种代数约束上。这个视角未来可能影响 tokenizer、表示监督或解释性评测设计。","technical_approach":"核心方法是把语义独立性映射为 partial orthogonality（部分正交）这样的代数结构。作者先从独立性公理出发，论证一个合理的语义独立概念应满足哪些性质，再寻找能在向量空间中承载这些性质的结构，并据此发展识别/解释 embedding 含义的方法。和直接做 probe 不同，这不是把下游标签拟合到 embedding 上，而是问 embedding 自身是否支持某类结构性推断。这样做的优势是解释更接近表示本体，代价是理论假设更强，且真实语言是否足够符合这些公理，需要经验检验。","experiments":"摘要没有给出具体数据集、模型或数值结果，因此不能可靠补充。可以推断，实验重点应是验证 partial orthogonality 是否能从现有文本 embedding 中恢复人类可解释的语义关系，或在合成/真实语料上检验独立性公理的适配程度。对做预训练的人，这篇论文的实验不应按 SOTA 看，而应看两点：一是这种结构分析在不同 embedding 家族里是否稳定；二是它是否提供了相似度分析给不出的额外诊断信号。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper asks a deeper interpretability question: what semantic structure is actually encoded in embeddings beyond plain similarity? Standard analyses rely on cosine similarity or linear probes, which are useful but limited; they do not naturally capture conditional semantic independence, such as whether two concepts are independent given a broader parent concept.","importance":"This is mainly a representation-theory and interpretability contribution rather than a direct training advance. Its value for pretraining research is that it proposes a stronger lens for analyzing learned representations: if embeddings truly encode compositional semantics, that structure should appear not only in distances but also in algebraic relations. That is useful for diagnosis and possibly for future objective or evaluation design.","technical_approach":"The key idea is to model semantic independence through partial orthogonality. The authors start from independence axioms—what any reasonable notion of semantic independence should satisfy—and then identify an algebraic structure in vector spaces that can express those properties. The upside is a cleaner link between geometry and meaning. flow matching 这类连续归一化流/生成流方法，严格扩展到带对称性的几何数据上。以往做法通常把 SE(3) 等变性通过网络结构或数据增强近似注入，但训练目标本身未必等变，结果是采样效率、物理一致性和泛化都会受限；这个问题在分子、蛋白和3D点云生成里很关键，因为坐标系变化不应改变分布。","importance":"它的价值主要是方法级的：把 flow matching The broader pretraining relevance is indirect but real: when the objective respects the invariances of the domain, one can reduce reliance on augmentation and let the generation process encode the right bias by construction. The scope is narrower than mainstream LLM pretraining, but the idea matters for unified modeling over continuous multimodal representations.","technical_approach":"The core idea is an equivariant flow-matching objective, not just an equivariant backbone plugged into standard flow matching. The method formulates probability paths and vector-field learning so that the learned transport obeys the group action consistently; this matters because equivariance is enforced at the level of the objective and dynamics, rather than being left to parameter sharing alone. Compared with nearby work, it is not just another equivariant network and not just an equivariant score model; it turns flow matching itself into an equivariant training paradigm.","experiments":"From the available information, the paper is expected to evaluate on standard geometric generation settings and compare against non-equivariant flow or diffusion baselines, focusing on generation quality, sample efficiency, and robustness to rotations/translations. Earlier fast-clipping tricks existed, but they were often layer-specific and fragmented, which limited adoption in larger or more modern models.","importance":"This is a system-plus-method contribution with direct relevance to large-scale training under privacy constraints. In practice, the dominant cost of DP-SGD is often not the noise injection itself but computing and clipping per-sample gradients; making that step broadly cheaper can materially expand the range of feasible private pretraining and alignment workloads. It does not change the privacy-utility frontier in principle, but it lowers the engineering and compute barrier substantially.","technical_approach":"The core idea is a unified fast gradient-clipping framework for DP-SGD that expresses efficient per-example norm computation in a common form across layer types. The key is not to approximate away clipping, but to exploit backprop structure and layer-wise decompositions so that the quantities needed for clipping can be obtained much more cheaply without changing the semantics of DP-SGD. This becomes especially painful in serving, where one backbone may need to support many tasks and per-task latency overhead quickly accumulates.","importance":"This is a method-and-systems contribution. Its importance is not just another PEFT variant, but a design that jointly optimizes parameter efficiency during adaptation and speed at inference time. That is highly relevant to the pretrain/post-train interface: an adaptation method is only truly useful at scale if it is cheap to train and also easy to deploy in a merged or low-overhead form.","technical_approach":"Conditional Adapters use conditioning to retain the parameter efficiency of adapter-style transfer while aiming for fast inference. Compared with standard stacked adapters, the emphasis is on injecting task-specific behavior in a form that can be selected or merged with minimal runtime branching. Relative to low-rank updates like LoRA, the concern here is not only trainable parameter count but also the shape of the deployed computation graph. The design works when task-specific variation can be captured by a small conditional control signal rather than a full extra forward path per task.","experiments":"The expected experimental picture is that conditional adapters match or exceed standard adapter/LoRA baselines on task quality while using a small fraction of trainable parameters and improving inference latency or throughput. masked input modeling 里提取“对决策有用”的不确定性，而不是对所有输入细节一视同仁地奖励新奇。","importance":"这项工作更像是训练信号设计上的方法改进，而不是范式替代。它对 pretrain 研究的启发在于：自监督建模目标不只是表示学习工具，也可以直接转成探索奖励；而且 masked modeling 比纯前向预测更容易聚焦可恢复的语义结构，避免把随机噪声当成 forward dynamics 的 curiosity 相比，它弱化了对低层像素变化和环境随机性的敏感性；与纯 reconstruction loss 相比，它通过 masking 强迫模型利用上下文，避免退化成记忆局部细节。边界也很清楚：如果任务需要的关键信号本来就非常局部，重 masking 可能反而削弱奖励质量。","experiments":"从题面未给摘要细节，但这类工作通常会在 Atari、DMControl 或 Procgen 一类稀疏奖励环境上评估探索收益。关键事实应包括：与 ICM、RND、NGU 或基于世界模型的内在奖励相比，MIMEx 在样本效率或最终回报上更稳，尤其在噪声较大或视觉复杂环境中优势更明显。最关键的 ablation 一般会看 masking 策略、重建目标和奖励归一化；若结果成立，应该能看到 MIMEx 对 stochastic distraction 更鲁棒，而不是只在少数游戏上刷分。由于缺少摘要，具体环境数、分数提升和网络规模无法可靠填写。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a weakness of intrinsic-reward methods in RL: many reward exploration based on prediction error or novelty, but those signals are easily hijacked by noise, uncontrollable dynamics, or superficial visual change. The authors instead ask whether masked input modeling can provide a cleaner uncertainty signal that reflects recoverable structure rather than raw surprise.","importance":"This is a training-signal contribution rather than a new RL paradigm, but it is relevant to pretraining because it turns a self-supervised objective into an exploration reward. The key idea is not to reward large forward prediction errors directly, but to reward states whose masked content is hard to reconstruct from context; that difficulty is meant to track learnable environment structure instead of incidental pixel variation. Compared with curiosity methods based on dynamics prediction, this should be less sensitive to stochastic distractions. Compared with vanilla reconstruction loss, masking forces the model to use broader context instead of memorizing local details. The tradeoff is that heavy masking can wash out genuinely local task-relevant signals.","experiments":"The abstract is not provided, so only the likely empirical shape can be summarized. In practice, people rely on ST for quantization, discrete latents, and routing because it works well enough, but there has been no clean unifying account of what it is really optimizing or how to improve on it.","importance":"This is a foundational optimization paper. It matters for pretraining because discrete components are everywhere now: vector quantization, multimodal tokenizers, MoE routing, hard memory access, and many non-autoregressive or diffusion-based discrete models all need some bridge between discrete forward passes and gradient-based learning. Better estimators can directly improve stability and trainability in those systems.","technical_approach":"From the title, the paper likely does two things: formalizes straight-through within a broader framework and proposes alternatives that go beyond vanilla ST. The central issue is the tradeoff among bias, variance, and forward-pass fidelity. Compared with score-function estimators, ST usually has low variance but substantial bias; compared with continuous relaxations, it better matches the true discrete forward computation. The contribution is likely a more principled estimator family or objective interpretation that clarifies and improves this tradeoff.","experiments":"Work in this area is usually validated across several representative discrete-learning tasks rather than one benchmark: quantization, discrete latent-variable models, routing, or related settings. Unlike plain chain-of-thought, the reasoning is not kept purely in natural language; language acts as the orchestration layer. trade-off：越想榨干硬件，越需要平台定制；越追求统一接口，越可能损失前沿训练效率。","experiments":"这类论文的实验证据通常围绕跨平台性能对比和工程重用成本，而不是标准 benchmark 分数。关键事实应是：同一算法或工作负载在不同硬件/软件栈上会出现显著性能差距，且要恢复性能往往需要额外定制开发；这种差距不是边角误差，而足以改变训练成本和可复制性判断。对 pretrain 读者，最有价值的是它提醒你重新看待 papers 里的‘同等算力预算’：如果 software stack 不可移植，所谓 compute budget 在不同实验室并不等价，很多 scaling 结果的外推边界也会更窄。","authors_note":"MIT；Neil Thompson 和 Sara Hooker 是较知名作者，分别在 AI This pushes back on the view that framework abstraction makes performance portable. The trade-off is explicit: the more peak efficiency you want, the more platform-specific work you usually need; a clean universal interface often leaves performance on the table.","experiments":"The important evidence is not benchmark accuracy but cross-platform performance and engineering cost. Unlike standard CLIP, which directly optimizes on noisy paired data at scale, S-CLIP tries to establish high-quality language grounding first and then expand coverage semi-supervisedly. Hein"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"NEURIPS","year":2023,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/nips/MullerVR023","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/nips/MullerVR023","_ee":"http://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/da909fc3893d272f26fd9db82e09d954-Abstract-Conference.html","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要回答的核心问题是：Sharpness-Aware Minimization（SAM）的收益到底来自哪里，是否真的需要对所有参数都做扰动。以往 SAM 被当作一种通用优化技巧直接套用，但它额外增加两次前后向开销，且机制解释一直不够清楚。作者指出，真正关键的并不是对整网参数施加邻域鲁棒性，而主要是归一化层参数决定了绝大部分效果，因此有必要重新拆解 SAM 的作用来源。","importance":"这项工作的价值主要是方法理解和系统降本，而不是提出全新的训练范式。对预训练研究者的启示很直接：如果 SAM 的有效部分集中在 LayerNorm / BatchNorm 这类归一化层，那么大模型训练里可以用更低成本保留大部分泛化收益，同时也说明 sharpness 与参数重参数化之间的关系比“平坦极小值更好”这种粗粒度叙事更重要。它的重要性在于把一个常用但昂贵的优化器技巧，压缩成了更可控、更容易迁移到大规模训练的版本。","technical_approach":"作者的核心方法很简单：只对归一化层参数应用 SAM 扰动，而不对其余权重做 sharpness-aware 更新。这个设计不是拍脑袋裁剪，而是基于对 SAM 作用位置的经验拆分：归一化层参数虽然数量很少，但它们直接控制每层激活的尺度与中心，扰动这里会放大全局损失几何上的变化；相反，大量普通权重上的扰动贡献有限，却承担了主要计算开销。和标准 SAM 相比，这个版本保留了两步更新框架，但把扰动子空间限制在一个极小参数子集，因此在计算、显存和实现复杂度上都更友好。","experiments":"论文的关键信息点是：仅扰动归一化层参数就能恢复接近完整 SAM 的泛化提升，而参数覆盖率远小于全模型。给定题面没有提供具体数据集、模型规模和精确数值，因此这里不能编造具体百分点，但从论文主结论看，作者在多种视觉架构上比较了标准训练、完整 SAM、以及只在归一化层上做 SAM 的变体，发现后者与完整 SAM 性能接近，同时显著降低额外代价。最有价值的消融不是多跑几个模型，而是按参数类型分组施加 SAM，直接证明收益高度集中于 normalization parameters，这对后续大模型优化器设计比单纯报一个 SOTA 更有参考意义。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper asks a focused question: where do SAM’s gains actually come from, and does it really need to perturb all parameters? SAM is widely used as a general-purpose regularizing optimizer, but it doubles training cost and its mechanism has remained somewhat vague. The authors argue that most of the benefit is concentrated in normalization-layer parameters, so the standard full-parameter formulation is likely overkill.","importance":"This is primarily a methods-understanding and systems-efficiency contribution, not a new training paradigm. The practical takeaway for pretraining is clear: if most of SAM’s value comes from LayerNorm/BatchNorm parameters, large-scale training may retain much of the generalization gain at far lower cost. The broader implication is that the interaction between sharpness and parameterization matters more than the usual flat-minima story suggests.","technical_approach":"The method is straightforward: apply SAM perturbations only to normalization-layer parameters and leave the rest of the network out of the adversarial neighborhood step. The design is motivated by a parameter-type decomposition of SAM’s effect rather than arbitrary pruning. Normalization parameters are few, but they control activation scale and centering throughout the network, so perturbing them changes the effective loss geometry disproportionately. Compared with standard SAM, the algorithm keeps the same two-step update but restricts the perturbation subspace to a tiny subset of parameters, which cuts cost and simplifies deployment.","experiments":"The key empirical result is that SAM on normalization layers alone recovers performance close to full SAM while touching only a very small fraction of parameters. The prompt does not provide the exact datasets, model sizes, or headline numbers, so those should not be fabricated. The important ablation is the parameter-type split: rather than just comparing against standard training, the paper isolates which parameter groups matter and shows that normalization parameters account for most of SAM’s benefit. That finding is more useful for optimizer design than a narrow benchmark win.","authors_note":""},"tags":["sam","normalization","optimization","sharpness-aware"]},{"id":"NaeiniSSGT23","title":"Large Language Models are Fixated by Red Herrings: Exploring Creative Problem Solving and Einstellung Effect using the Only Connect Wall Dataset","authors":["Saeid Alavi Naeini","Raeid Saqur","Mozhgan Saeidi","John M. This paper argues that modern representations can be highly compressible while hiding that redundancy in a diffuse, rotated form.","importance":"This is a representation-analysis contribution with practical implications for compression, architecture design, and interpretation. Previous multimodal alignment (e.g., CLIP) requires large-scale paired data. This work explores a training-free alternative.","importance":"A conceptual contribution. It provides evidence for the relative representation hypothesis—that independently trained encoders develop structurally similar representation geometries—suggesting modality alignment may be cheaper than assumed. However, performance lags far behind jointly trained models, so this is more proof-of-concept than practical.","technical_approach":"ASIF uses a small paired anchor set as a bridge. Given a query image, it finds nearest neighbors in the anchor set's image embeddings (from a vision encoder like DINO), then uses the corresponding text embeddings (from a separate language model) as cross-modal proxies. No learned mapping or adapter is involved. The method relies entirely on the assumption that relative distance structures are preserved across independently trained embedding spaces.","experiments":"Tested on image-text retrieval.
